threads.subject: Re: [Phd-students] [Faculty] [Columbia HCI] Congratulations to Dr. Gaurav Jain!
threads.body: Congrats Dr. Jain!
threads.snippet: Congrats Dr. Jain!
threads.originalBody:
It is my great pleasure to announce that Dr. Gaurav Jain has successfully defended his dissertation, "Enabling Agency in Access to Visual Experiences for Blind Users"!

Our field today is abuzz with AI agents. In an exciting new direction, Gaurav showed how AI can be used to give *people* a greater sense of agency and perception rather than AI acting as the agent itself. Gaurav's work led to the concept of "exploration assistance systems" that help users perceive and explore the world around them, including better interpreting digital media such as sports broadcasts.

Gaurav is starting as a research scientist at Meta in their NYC office next month, creating better recommendations and viewing experiences for Instagram Reels. Gaurav has mentored a whopping 17 undergraduate and MS co-authors during his time here, including REU students and SURE students. Our lab wishes Gaurav the very best in his next chapter.

Congratulations, Dr. Jain!

--Brian

threads.subject: Re: [Phd-students] [Faculty] Congratulations to Dr. Kelly Kostopoulou!
threads.body: Congrats Dr. Kostopoulou!!!
threads.snippet: Congrats Dr. Kostopoulou!!!
threads.originalBody:
Congrats to Kelly for defending her thesis, entitled: "Optimizing Privacy Budget Management in Differentially Private Systems".

Kelly's thesis explores how to design practical differentially-private systems. Her work takes theoretical differentially privacy mechanisms, and implements them for different use cases (job scheduling, databases, ad serving) on real-world systems. In the process of doing so, her work uncovers (and solves) fundamental and novel systems challenges, such as: how to efficiently cache differentially private database queries? How to efficiently manage the differential privacy budget resource across multiple queries? Across multiple users?

Kelly's work has not only appeared in top systems conferences, but has also made a real-world impact. For example, her work on how to efficiently serve online ads while protecting user privacy (together with co-conspirators Pierre Tholoniat and Roxana Geambasu), is in the process of being standardized and rolled out to major browsers (e.g., Firefox).

Kelly is joining Meta's systems research group, where she will be working on problems related to large scale resource management. I'm both happy and sad that she is graduating. We will really miss you Kelly!

Onwards and upwards!
Asaf

Hi All,

You still have one or more tasks to complete before being cleared to pick up your graduation tickets. Please handle these ASAP because a few more hours today and tomorrow afternoon are the last times for ticket pick-up.

Journalism School Graduation tickets are now available for pick-up. Each ticket envelope (with your name on it) will have your student ticket and tickets for your guests for the Journalism School May Graduation Ceremony.

In order to pick up your tickets, you must FIRST have completed the following:
1. assessment submissions (M.S.)

threads.snippet: I do not see an option for Digital Key in my inspire app. Can you please advise?
threads.originalBody:
Dear Valued Resident,

Silver Towers has invited you to a new way to simplify your everyday access using resident keys in your Apple Wallet!

It's so easy! You can now use your iPhone or Apple Watch instead of your physical key for easy, secure, and private access to Silver Towers and your apartment.

There are a few simple steps that are required for you to begin taking advantage of this time saving and cutting-edge feature.

The lock on your door will need a quick upgrade that will take no more than 5-10 minutes to complete, but we will need your confirmation to make that upgrade. Please respond to this e-mail so we can get your lock updated.

Be sure to have downloaded the Inspire Your Day App and create an account.

Within the Inspire App, click on your profile icon (or initials) to open the account menu. From you will see a new menu option called "Digital Key".

From that screen, to add your digital key, click on "Add to Apple Wallet" and follow the screen instructions. Once added to your Apple Wallet, please wait 5 minutes for the lock to be synced before using it for the first time. The card can be used even when the phone is locked.

If you have any additional questions, please respond to this e-mail, or use the support button located at the bottom of the Inspire App.

Thank you!

Silver Towers Property Management Team

threads.subject: Re: The paper looks good
threads.body:
Hi Jeff,

Thanks for your comments! Lydia is back online, she's working on the discussion. I hopefully should have the rest ready for a review before 9:30.

Thanks,
Karthik
threads.snippet: Thanks for your comments! Lydia is back online, she's working on the discussion.
threads.originalBody:
My advice at this stage is to get it rapidly to a good enough stage to submit. That means either completing open sections or deciding not to include them, with a bias toward the latter, given the paper is fairly long.

I think you are using the red coding for sections to come back to later. Once you have the empty sections either complete or deleted, I think you can start resolving the red sections, in most cases by simply turning them black.

Then you will something good enough, and can go through and figure out small changes that will make the paper read better – for example, checking to see that similar concepts use similar language to describe them. I can do a read-through after I teach – say at 9:30 – to proofread and maybe suggest some small changes. I understand Lydia will also be looking at it then. I think you will want there to be only a few moving parts by then – most of the paper should be stable, to avoid wild gyrations before submission.

threads.originalBody:
Dear authors,

IUI'25 proceedings chairs here.

We are reaching out to you because we noticed that while your submission is fully uploaded on PCS, the TAPS process has not yet been completed (100%). As a result, the PDF versions on PCS and TAPS do not match.

The issues might include one of the following:
- PDF versions in PCS and TAPS are the same, but TAPS is at 75% (you need to accept the version)
- pending tickets from TAPS support
- empty submissions on both PCS and TAPS

We kindly ask you to finish the TAPS process to 100% and upload the resulting PDF version on PCS as soon as possible and no later than February 5th (strict deadline).

If you face any problems on TAPS or uploading the PDF on PCS, please let us know immediately.

Best,
Federico and Maximiliane
– IUI'25 Proceedings Chairs –

Hi Hayden,

I'll be moving on May 31st, so I've filled out the forms you listed with my new address. Please let me know if there is anything else you need from me or if I need to do anything differently with these forms.

Thanks,
Karthik
threads.snippet: I'll be moving on May 31st, so I've filled out the forms you listed with my new address.
threads.originalBody:
Good Morning,

I'm happy to announce that I have been assigned to help process your Summer '24 hiring documents. The good news is that since you are currently active in PAC, all I will need from you are the following:

   1. Please confirm if your address has changed or if it will remain the same. If it has changed, please complete the attached tax forms and return them to me.

   - Casual Employment Form
      - Form W-4
      - Form IT-2104 (If applicable: Form IT2104.1 or IT2104E)

            2. Please log in to TLAM via PAC and begin to submit your hours worked for the Summer '24 semester.

Please let me know if you have any questions.

Thank you,

Hayden Wander | School of Engineering and Applied Science | Columbia University | hw2942@columbia.edu
Office Phone: 212-853-8483

He/They - Why is this here? https://universitylife.columbia.edu/pronouns

Confidentiality Notice:

The information contained in the email message is intended only for the personal and confidential use of the recipient(s) named above.  If the reader of this message is not the intended recipient or an agent responsible for delivering it to the intended recipient, you are hereby notified that you have received this document in error and that any review, dissemination, or copying of this message is strictly prohibited.  If you have received this communication in error, please notify me immediately by email, and delete the original message.

threads.subject: Re: Last 2 assignments
threads.body:
Hi Wonders,

I thought I'd explained in my past emails - but future assignments are just iterations. I left some feedback on your latest submission, if you can turn that around I'll give one more round of feedback before you submit a final video. Does that make sense/work?

Thanks,
Karthik
threads.snippet: I thought I'd explained in my past emails - but future assignments are just iterations.
threads.originalBody:
Hi Karthik,

I hope this email finds you well. I heard that you've been under the weather lately, and I just wanted to reach out to express my sympathy and wish you a speedy recovery.

I understand that there are still two assignments outstanding, and I wanted to inquire about what will happen with these. Could you please provide some guidance on how we can proceed with these assignments?

Thank you for your attention, and I look forward to your response.

Best regards,

Wonders

threads.body:
Hi Sophia,

Does the week of the 25th work for you?

Thanks,
Karthik
threads.snippet: Does the week of the 25th work for you?
threads.originalBody:
Hi there,

This is Sophia Burnette from New York Tennis League. I wanted to reach out to set up a time for a doubles match with my partner Morgan. We both played varsity in high school and are looking to get back into it - probably both a 3.0-3.25. We are free Tuesday after 7:30PM, or Monday, Wednesdays, and Thursdays anytime after 6:30PM. Please let us know if you want to set up a time to play. Looking forward to hearing from you!

threads.body:
Hi Layanne,

I can meet with you tomorrow, but I'm afraid it's too late for make up.

I see that I missed one email from you - but given there was no follow up on email/slack I had assumed you had dropped the class since you did not turn in any assignments nor attend any of the sections (which were originally announced via coursework).

Let me know when works best for you tomorrow.

Thanks,
Karthik

Dear Karthik,

Thank you for your interest in participating in our study. Please review
the study instructions and the procedure here
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__drive.google.com_file_d_1cLjaV-2DgeubXdjf-5FDfC2kj9-5FgUt1QntM-5F_view-3Fusp-3Dsharing&d=DwIBaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=NWVoBYbZXE4exu99cxrBfc4y-4lCqiQFhTs6lGpPKrfc-wb1RYVIltdzhJOwfsXT&s=tYsQchVP07uZBFHQ1ENz80_y0dlTMXy5nnx6JXH0rIM&e= >.
Once you have read through the study instructions and signed the consent
form <https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__forms.gle_gh9nBm8vFZwHhPRg7&d=DwIBaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=NWVoBYbZXE4exu99cxrBfc4y-4lCqiQFhTs6lGpPKrfc-wb1RYVIltdzhJOwfsXT&s=Vp61nC8Mp-0gz1oWyN-2NZHl8KGUuR38O5qSmgpSsM0&e= >, kindly respond to this email
thread so that I can share the quiz website with you. You will be able to
complete the quiz at your convenience.

If you have any questions or concerns about the study or its design, please
feel free to reach out.

Best regards,

Anubhav

CS PhD student
Columbia University
Website <https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__anubhav-2Djangra.github.io_&d=DwIBaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=NWVoBYbZXE4exu99cxrBfc4y-4lCqiQFhTs6lGpPKrfc-wb1RYVIltdzhJOwfsXT&s=quvRhb7VQwssZ0FZaQRVKbopE8F3a2TAun0Lb-g8U-s&e= > | Email
<anubhav@cs.columbia.edu>

threads.subject: Re: [tickets.cs.columbia.edu #202359] Re: Fall 2025 New GRA Appointment
threads.body: Apologize for misspelling your name!
threads.originalBody:
Dear Students,

Welcome to Computer Science!

You will soon receive your appointment letter and hiring packet via
DocuSign.

While the packet contains detailed instructions, we want to highlight a few
important steps:

   1.

   Social Security Number (SSN)
   If you need to apply for an SSN, please see the attached instructions.
   Because it can take time to receive your Social Security receipt (required
   for processing your hiring paperwork), we have prepared the On-Campus
   Employer Letter for you in advance (Step 2). These letters are ready for
   pickup at the Computer Science Office (Computer Science Building, Suite
   450) Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM–5:00 PM.

   2.

   I-9 Verification
   You must complete both Part I (online) and Part II (in person), as this
   step authorizes you to work. Please refer to the attached instructions.

Please complete all parts of your hiring paperwork and submit it as soon as
possible, but no later than Friday, August 29, 2025.

If there are any changes or if you have been approved to receive a
fellowship recently, please let us know as soon as possible so we can
forward you relevant guidance and documentation.

Please don’t hesitate to let us know if you have any questions by emailing
student-payroll@cs.columbia.edu. We will also be available in person to
answer questions during the PhD Orientation on Monday, August 25, 2025.

Thank you,

Student Payroll

threads.subject: Re: Welcome New CS PhD Students
threads.body:
Hi Rob,

I hope you're doing well! I apologize for the delay in reaching out, but
I've been having trouble setting up my CS account with an alias. I've
attached screenshots of the form - if I put in anything other than my uni
in the username field, it says that it cannot be blank. I'd appreciate
guidance with how to proceed with this, thanks for your help!

Thanks,
Karthik
threads.originalBody:
Dear New PhD Students,

I am the Director of IT for the Department of Computer Science. The CS IT
team is Computing Research Facilities (CRF) and we will be working with you
during your time here.

We can be reached at crf@cs.columbia.edu.

Please read over the following items, especially the first one regarding CS
account creation.

1. Your Computer Science Account

We need to create your CS account.  Please note that if you were here
previously and already have an account with the same name as your UNI, you
should still apply for a new one and we will transfer your home directory
over.

Your account name can be whatever you like as long as it is available.

I’d like to encourage you to choose an account name that is both readable
and professional, keeping in mind that it will also be used for your CS
email (see item 2).  You can use your name (such as nancysmith or nsmith)
as long as it is not already in use.

Account request page: https://www.cs.columbia.edu/crf/account-form/ 

Notes on a few of the fields.

    Sponsor: Choose your advisor from the drop-down list.
    Account Type: Select "CS PhD Student."
    Username: Your choice.  We will contact you if it is unavailable.
    Password: Please choose a good one!

2. Your Computer Science Email

When your CS account has been set up I will submit a ticket to CUIT
(Columbia University IT) to have your CS email address created.  It will be
of the format username@cs.columbia.edu and will be an alias for your UNI
email address so you will be able to use the two of them interchangeably.

Columbia's mail service is called LionMail and runs on Google.  Information
about LionMail can be found here: https://cuit.columbia.edu/email/lionmail 

3. VPN account

Please follow the instructions on the CRF website if you need to use the
VPN (you must have a CS account first).

http://www.cs.columbia.edu  --> CRF (bottom of page) --> VPN

CUIT also provides a VPN service.

https://www.cuit.columbia.edu/remote-access-services 

4. Printing

Even if you already have installed a CS printer as an undergraduate or MS
student, please install it again using your CS PhD student account. There
is no printing limit for PhD student accounts. Please do not use the CS
printers for personal bulk print purposes such as printing flyers or books
that are not related to your research!

http://www.cs.columbia.edu  --> CRF -> Printers

5. Have a question or request?

Please send all your questions or requests via email to our ticketing
system:

    crf@cs.columbia.edu

If you require immediate assistance you can call the CRF hotline at
212-853-8426.

I think that's it. I will be stopping by the orientation meeting this
afternoon if you have any questions.

Welcome to the Computer Science department!  If you were previously a
student here, welcome back!

Regards,

Rob.

-- 
Rob Lane
Executive Director of Information Technology
Department of Computer Science, Columbia University
(212) 853-8402

messages.subject: Welcome New CS PhD Students
messages.body:
Dear New PhD Students,

I am the Director of IT for the Department of Computer Science. The CS IT
team is Computing Research Facilities (CRF) and we will be working with you
during your time here.

We can be reached at crf@cs.columbia.edu.

Please read over the following items, especially the first one regarding CS
account creation.

1. Your Computer Science Account

We need to create your CS account.  Please note that if you were here
previously and already have an account with the same name as your UNI, you
should still apply for a new one and we will transfer your home directory
over.

Your account name can be whatever you like as long as it is available.

I’d like to encourage you to choose an account name that is both readable
and professional, keeping in mind that it will also be used for your CS
email (see item 2).  You can use your name (such as nancysmith or nsmith)
as long as it is not already in use.

Account request page: https://www.cs.columbia.edu/crf/account-form/ 

Notes on a few of the fields.

    Sponsor: Choose your advisor from the drop-down list.
    Account Type: Select "CS PhD Student."
    Username: Your choice.  We will contact you if it is unavailable.
    Password: Please choose a good one!

2. Your Computer Science Email

When your CS account has been set up I will submit a ticket to CUIT
(Columbia University IT) to have your CS email address created.  It will be
of the format username@cs.columbia.edu and will be an alias for your UNI
email address so you will be able to use the two of them interchangeably.

threads.subject: Re: LLM-based social simulations
threads.body: That's great, does anytime Tuesday after 2:30PM work for everyone?
threads.originalBody:
Hi, Karthik, Lydia, Henry Lieberman, Research Scienist at MIT CSAIL, here. We had some conversations about your work in social simulations using LLMs, at IUI in Sardinia. You mentioned that you have a toolkit for doing such simulations and were
ZjQcmQRYFpfptBannerStart
This Message Is From an External Sender
This message came from outside your organization.
 
ZjQcmQRYFpfptBannerEnd
Hi, Karthik, Lydia,

Henry Lieberman, Research Scienist at MIT CSAIL, here. We had some 
conversations about your work in social simulations using LLMs, at IUI 
in Sardinia.

You mentioned that you have a toolkit for doing such simulations and 
were interested in collaborations.  I have some students and colleagues 
who are interested in working in this area, and we'd like to get a 
collaboration going. If you're willing to share your toolkit, it would 
be tremendously useful for us. We'd also be excited to learn of your 
future research directions and if there's any way we could help.

Some of the ideas we have in mind are:

* Variants on the Prisoner's Dilemma. In your IUI paper, you did a great 
simulation of the Prisoner's Dilemma along classic lines. We would like 
to experiment with variants of the rules, in order to arrive at 
scenarios that are more favorable to the evolution of cooperation. These 
might involve:
   - Relaxing the "no communication" rule between participants.
   - Modeling the long-term "wealth" of participants. We want to 
establish a relationship between scarcity/abundance and 
defection/cooperation. Wealthier participants can better afford to risk 
the chance of defection by partners, and so can reap a risk/reward 
benefit. So cooperating can actually be an "investment in the future", 
because it makes it more likely that your future partners will 
cooperate. Poorer participants would be unable to invest or take risk of 
loss.
   - Relaxing the "maximize score" goal.  Though technically the IPD is 
not a zero-sum game, any game with multiple players who are all trying 
to maximize their score makes it zero-sum in relation to the 
maximization goal (If I maximize, you can't). We would replace this with 
"satisficing" goals.
   - Being able to give individual participants different 
"personalities" or "strategies" that allow us to model heterogenous 
games, and condition results on the distribution of players with various 
strategies.

* Simulation of economic systems.  Our book, "Why Can't We All Just Get 
Along?" (https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.whycantwe.org&d=DwIDaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=434tGpy832-t3sYSYVcFqbdG-DHekr4UgHkBnu1IvxQY4TLH1P9OyeoNglzMCvM3&s=j4xJW6sQR43tUtIWVvbW4CNuHZB_P7qt8d7sfkDd5zE&e=) paints a vision of a post-scarcity 
future where universal abundance brought about by AI, personal 
manufacturing and biotech, promotes cooperation in social systems.  We 
propose a new economic principle called Makerism, based on more 
decentralized production made possible by technology. We would like to 
do simulations of Makerism vs. conventional Capitalism (or 
Communism/Socialism). We think we can debunk conventional arguments 
about "economies of scale" and "incentives", in the face of 
technological progress.  Please see our site, where you will find my 
12-minute TED talk as a quick introduction, and you may download the 
book free with coupon code: makerism. Please see the chapter on 
Makerism, Chapter 15.

* Education. We are working on a new interactive, "articulate book" 
edition of Why Can't We, and we'd like to link to online simulations 
that lets readers play interactively with the ideas. An LLM chatbot 
would be ideal as a simulation controller. We've also trained an LLM 
with the book content so that it can interactively answer questions and 
have conversations about the book ideas.

I'd like to introduce you to Aditya Mehta, cc'ed, who is an economist 
working with me.  He also has some economic ideas about strategies for 
small businesses who must compete with larger businesses that could also 
be tested with this methodology. If we could get Aditya started by 
direct contact with Karthik, he could see if we could get some of your 
tools going.  We also can set up a Zoom meeting to get acquainted.

We'd love to hear what you think about all this, and look forward to the 
possibility of working with you.

Best,

Henry Lieberman

Research Scientist
Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Lab (CSAIL)
Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT)
32 Vassar St. G 498
Cambridge, MA 02139 USA
https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=http-3A__www.media.mit.edu_-7Elieber&d=DwIDaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=434tGpy832-t3sYSYVcFqbdG-DHekr4UgHkBnu1IvxQY4TLH1P9OyeoNglzMCvM3&s=IkAZiHaDeT63kBtMNzUwelKuB7tJUXxtf2twsT3GBH4&e=
(1-617) 388-9612

messages.subject: Re: LLM-based social simulations
messages.body:
Awesome! Maybe we could do a quick meeting to learn more and figure out
what technical stuff we can help with, and go from there. How’s next week
for you?

We do have the CHI deadline sept 11th… so there we’ll probably be a little
preoccupied that week. But otherwise would love to catch up.

You’ve definitely convinced me that Prisoners dilemma is crucial to
figuring out how to engender cooperation :) excited to learn more!

messages.subject: Re: LLM-based social simulations
messages.body:
Lydia, Karthik,

I hope you're having a good first week of school, which is busy for us 
as well.

I propose Friday 12 September. Lydia, are you in New York or in Eastern 
Europe? If you're in NYC, best would be 4pm.
If in Europe, would that be too late? If so, how about 12 noon = 6 pm if 
that works for Aditya?

Looking forward to meeting with you guys.

Best,

Henry and Aditya

>     about "economies of scale" and "incentives", in the face of
>     technological progress.  Please see our site, where you will find my
>     12-minute TED talk as a quick introduction, and you may download the
>     book free with coupon code: makerism. Please see the chapter on
>     Makerism, Chapter 15.
>
>     * Education. We are working on a new interactive, "articulate book"
>     edition of Why Can't We, and we'd like to link to online simulations
>     that lets readers play interactively with the ideas. An LLM chatbot
>     would be ideal as a simulation controller. We've also trained an LLM
>     with the book content so that it can interactively answer questions and
>     have conversations about the book ideas.
>
>     I'd like to introduce you to Aditya Mehta, cc'ed, who is an economist
>     working with me.  He also has some economic ideas about strategies for
>     small businesses who must compete with larger businesses that could also
>     be tested with this methodology. If we could get Aditya started by
>     direct contact with Karthik, he could see if we could get some of your
>     tools going.  We also can set up a Zoom meeting to get acquainted.
>
>     We'd love to hear what you think about all this, and look forward to the
>     possibility of working with you.
>
>     Best,
>
>     Henry Lieberman
>
>     Research Scientist
>     Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Lab (CSAIL)
>     Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT)
>     32 Vassar St. G 498
>     Cambridge, MA 02139 USA
>     https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=http-3A__www.media.mit.edu_-7Elieber&d=DwIDaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=hPTJFKhz3Hkwikm3mM2701VB1AZoIWoRMgOYWA3SHD0&m=8mLmh9S_7zIaXAlpXG8dQE1pLEwQ-OwkgM1j_1jxmwayBJV6z977sikG64WHWRjR&s=arl4x4UGc0Sq0vGBe3ECgzMxK5CXJwQVSaLkpgxGBRs&e= <https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=http-3A__www.media.mit.edu_-7Elieber&d=DwIDaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=hPTJFKhz3Hkwikm3mM2701VB1AZoIWoRMgOYWA3SHD0&m=8mLmh9S_7zIaXAlpXG8dQE1pLEwQ-OwkgM1j_1jxmwayBJV6z977sikG64WHWRjR&s=arl4x4UGc0Sq0vGBe3ECgzMxK5CXJwQVSaLkpgxGBRs&e=>

messages.subject: Re: LLM-based social simulations
messages.body:
On 8/27/25 8:15 PM, Lydia Chilton wrote:
> Awesome! Maybe we could do a quick meeting to learn more and figure 
> out what technical stuff we can help with, and go from there. How’s 
> next week for you?
>
> We do have the CHI deadline sept 11th… so there we’ll probably be a 
> little preoccupied that week. But otherwise would love to catch up.
>
> You’ve definitely convinced me that Prisoners dilemma is crucial to 
> figuring out how to engender cooperation :) excited to learn more!
>
> On Wed, Aug 27, 2025 at 13:49 Henry Lieberman <henry@csail.mit.edu> wrote:
>
>     Hi, Karthik, Lydia, Henry Lieberman, Research Scienist at MIT
>     CSAIL, here. We had some conversations about your work in social
>     simulations using LLMs, at IUI in Sardinia. You mentioned that you
>     have a toolkit for doing such simulations and were
>     ZjQcmQRYFpfptBannerStart
>     This Message Is From an External Sender
>     This message came from outside your organization.
>     ZjQcmQRYFpfptBannerEnd
>
>     Hi, Karthik, Lydia,
>
>     Henry Lieberman, Research Scienist at MIT CSAIL, here. We had some
>     conversations about your work in social simulations using LLMs, at IUI
>     in Sardinia.
>
>     You mentioned that you have a toolkit for doing such simulations and
>     were interested in collaborations.  I have some students and colleagues
>     who are interested in working in this area, and we'd like to get a
>     collaboration going. If you're willing to share your toolkit, it would
>     be tremendously useful for us. We'd also be excited to learn of your
>     future research directions and if there's any way we could help.
>
>     Some of the ideas we have in mind are:
>
>     * Variants on the Prisoner's Dilemma. In your IUI paper, you did a great
>     simulation of the Prisoner's Dilemma along classic lines. We would like
>     to experiment with variants of the rules, in order to arrive at
>     scenarios that are more favorable to the evolution of cooperation. These
>     might involve:
>         - Relaxing the "no communication" rule between participants.
>         - Modeling the long-term "wealth" of participants. We want to
>     establish a relationship between scarcity/abundance and
>     defection/cooperation. Wealthier participants can better afford to risk
>     the chance of defection by partners, and so can reap a risk/reward
>     benefit. So cooperating can actually be an "investment in the future",
>     because it makes it more likely that your future partners will
>     cooperate. Poorer participants would be unable to invest or take risk of
>     loss.
>         - Relaxing the "maximize score" goal.  Though technically the IPD is
>     not a zero-sum game, any game with multiple players who are all trying
>     to maximize their score makes it zero-sum in relation to the
>     maximization goal (If I maximize, you can't). We would replace this with
>     "satisficing" goals.
>         - Being able to give individual participants different
>     "personalities" or "strategies" that allow us to model heterogenous
>     games, and condition results on the distribution of players with various
>     strategies.
>
>     * Simulation of economic systems.  Our book, "Why Can't We All Just Get
>     Along?" (https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.whycantwe.org&d=DwIDaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=hPTJFKhz3Hkwikm3mM2701VB1AZoIWoRMgOYWA3SHD0&m=8mLmh9S_7zIaXAlpXG8dQE1pLEwQ-OwkgM1j_1jxmwayBJV6z977sikG64WHWRjR&s=ByUllIWQLCU4I9U-XoUXoxpmSumMMnVmGGQxGRGnPdM&e= <https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.whycantwe.org&d=DwIDaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=hPTJFKhz3Hkwikm3mM2701VB1AZoIWoRMgOYWA3SHD0&m=8mLmh9S_7zIaXAlpXG8dQE1pLEwQ-OwkgM1j_1jxmwayBJV6z977sikG64WHWRjR&s=ByUllIWQLCU4I9U-XoUXoxpmSumMMnVmGGQxGRGnPdM&e=>) paints a vision of a post-scarcity
>     future where universal abundance brought about by AI, personal
>     manufacturing and biotech, promotes cooperation in social systems.  We
>     propose a new economic principle called Makerism, based on more
>     decentralized production made possible by technology. We would like to
>     do simulations of Makerism vs. conventional Capitalism (or
>     Communism/Socialism). We think we can debunk conventional arguments
>     about "economies of scale" and "incentives", in the face of
>     technological progress.  Please see our site, where you will find my
>     12-minute TED talk as a quick introduction, and you may download the
>     book free with coupon code: makerism. Please see the chapter on
>     Makerism, Chapter 15.
>
>     * Education. We are working on a new interactive, "articulate book"
>     edition of Why Can't We, and we'd like to link to online simulations
>     that lets readers play interactively with the ideas. An LLM chatbot
>     would be ideal as a simulation controller. We've also trained an LLM
>     with the book content so that it can interactively answer questions and
>     have conversations about the book ideas.
>
>     I'd like to introduce you to Aditya Mehta, cc'ed, who is an economist
>     working with me.  He also has some economic ideas about strategies for
>     small businesses who must compete with larger businesses that could also
>     be tested with this methodology. If we could get Aditya started by
>     direct contact with Karthik, he could see if we could get some of your
>     tools going.  We also can set up a Zoom meeting to get acquainted.
>
>     We'd love to hear what you think about all this, and look forward to the
>     possibility of working with you.
>
>     Best,
>
>     Henry Lieberman
>
>     Research Scientist
>     Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Lab (CSAIL)
>     Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT)
>     32 Vassar St. G 498
>     Cambridge, MA 02139 USA
>     https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=http-3A__www.media.mit.edu_-7Elieber&d=DwIDaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=hPTJFKhz3Hkwikm3mM2701VB1AZoIWoRMgOYWA3SHD0&m=8mLmh9S_7zIaXAlpXG8dQE1pLEwQ-OwkgM1j_1jxmwayBJV6z977sikG64WHWRjR&s=arl4x4UGc0Sq0vGBe3ECgzMxK5CXJwQVSaLkpgxGBRs&e= <https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=http-3A__www.media.mit.edu_-7Elieber&d=DwIDaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=hPTJFKhz3Hkwikm3mM2701VB1AZoIWoRMgOYWA3SHD0&m=8mLmh9S_7zIaXAlpXG8dQE1pLEwQ-OwkgM1j_1jxmwayBJV6z977sikG64WHWRjR&s=arl4x4UGc0Sq0vGBe3ECgzMxK5CXJwQVSaLkpgxGBRs&e=>

messages.body:
Hi All,

Would it be possible to do next week instead? I'm fairly free in the first
part of the week, I can do Monday after 11AM ET, Tuesday outside of 12PM to
2:30PM, and Wednesday outside of 1PM to 2PM if any of those times work for
everyone!

Thanks,
Karthik

messages.subject: Re: LLM-based social simulations
messages.body:
Karthik,

Yes, we can reschedule. Tuesday afternoon best, Monday iffy, Wednesday 
booked all day.

Henry

>>>             Hi, Karthik, Lydia,
>>>
>>>             Henry Lieberman, Research Scienist at MIT CSAIL, here. We had some
>>>             conversations about your work in social simulations using LLMs, at IUI
>>>             in Sardinia.
>>>
>>>             You mentioned that you have a toolkit for doing such simulations and
>>>             were interested in collaborations.  I have some students and colleagues
>>>             who are interested in working in this area, and we'd like to get a
>>>             collaboration going. If you're willing to share your toolkit, it would
>>>             be tremendously useful for us. We'd also be excited to learn of your
>>>             future research directions and if there's any way we could help.
>>>
>>>             Some of the ideas we have in mind are:
>>>
>>>             * Variants on the Prisoner's Dilemma. In your IUI paper, you did a great
>>>             simulation of the Prisoner's Dilemma along classic lines. We would like
>>>             to experiment with variants of the rules, in order to arrive at
>>>             scenarios that are more favorable to the evolution of cooperation. These
>>>             might involve:
>>>                 - Relaxing the "no communication" rule between participants.
>>>                 - Modeling the long-term "wealth" of participants. We want to
>>>             establish a relationship between scarcity/abundance and
>>>             defection/cooperation. Wealthier participants can better afford to risk
>>>             the chance of defection by partners, and so can reap a risk/reward
>>>             benefit. So cooperating can actually be an "investment in the future",
>>>             because it makes it more likely that your future partners will
>>>             cooperate. Poorer participants would be unable to invest or take risk of
>>>             loss.
>>>                 - Relaxing the "maximize score" goal.  Though technically the IPD is
>>>             not a zero-sum game, any game with multiple players who are all trying
>>>             to maximize their score makes it zero-sum in relation to the
>>>             maximization goal (If I maximize, you can't). We would replace this with
>>>             "satisficing" goals.
>>>                 - Being able to give individual participants different
>>>             "personalities" or "strategies" that allow us to model heterogenous
>>>             games, and condition results on the distribution of players with various
>>>             strategies.
>>>
>>>             * Simulation of economic systems.  Our book, "Why Can't We All Just Get
>>>             Along?" (https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.whycantwe.org&d=DwIDaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=hPTJFKhz3Hkwikm3mM2701VB1AZoIWoRMgOYWA3SHD0&m=8mLmh9S_7zIaXAlpXG8dQE1pLEwQ-OwkgM1j_1jxmwayBJV6z977sikG64WHWRjR&s=ByUllIWQLCU4I9U-XoUXoxpmSumMMnVmGGQxGRGnPdM&e= <https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.whycantwe.org&d=DwIDaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=hPTJFKhz3Hkwikm3mM2701VB1AZoIWoRMgOYWA3SHD0&m=8mLmh9S_7zIaXAlpXG8dQE1pLEwQ-OwkgM1j_1jxmwayBJV6z977sikG64WHWRjR&s=ByUllIWQLCU4I9U-XoUXoxpmSumMMnVmGGQxGRGnPdM&e=>) paints a vision of a post-scarcity
>>>             future where universal abundance brought about by AI, personal
>>>             manufacturing and biotech, promotes cooperation in social systems.  We
>>>             propose a new economic principle called Makerism, based on more
>>>             decentralized production made possible by technology. We would like to
>>>             do simulations of Makerism vs. conventional Capitalism (or
>>>             Communism/Socialism). We think we can debunk conventional arguments
>>>             about "economies of scale" and "incentives", in the face of
>>>             technological progress.  Please see our site, where you will find my
>>>             12-minute TED talk as a quick introduction, and you may download the
>>>             book free with coupon code: makerism. Please see the chapter on
>>>             Makerism, Chapter 15.
>>>
>>>             * Education. We are working on a new interactive, "articulate book"
>>>             edition of Why Can't We, and we'd like to link to online simulations
>>>             that lets readers play interactively with the ideas. An LLM chatbot
>>>             would be ideal as a simulation controller. We've also trained an LLM
>>>             with the book content so that it can interactively answer questions and
>>>             have conversations about the book ideas.
>>>
>>>             I'd like to introduce you to Aditya Mehta, cc'ed, who is an economist
>>>             working with me.  He also has some economic ideas about strategies for
>>>             small businesses who must compete with larger businesses that could also
>>>             be tested with this methodology. If we could get Aditya started by
>>>             direct contact with Karthik, he could see if we could get some of your
>>>             tools going.  We also can set up a Zoom meeting to get acquainted.
>>>
>>>             We'd love to hear what you think about all this, and look forward to the
>>>             possibility of working with you.
>>>
>>>             Best,
>>>
>>>             Henry Lieberman
>>>
>>>             Research Scientist
>>>             Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Lab (CSAIL)
>>>             Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT)
>>>             32 Vassar St. G 498

>>>>>> would be ideal as a simulation controller. We've also trained an LLM
>>>>>> with the book content so that it can interactively answer questions and
>>>>>> have conversations about the book ideas.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> I'd like to introduce you to Aditya Mehta, cc'ed, who is an economist
>>>>>> working with me.  He also has some economic ideas about strategies for
>>>>>> small businesses who must compete with larger businesses that could also
>>>>>> be tested with this methodology. If we could get Aditya started by
>>>>>> direct contact with Karthik, he could see if we could get some of your
>>>>>> tools going.  We also can set up a Zoom meeting to get acquainted.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> We'd love to hear what you think about all this, and look forward to the
>>>>>> possibility of working with you.
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Best,
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Henry Lieberman
>>>>>>
>>>>>> Research Scientist
>>>>>> Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Lab (CSAIL)
>>>>>> Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT)
>>>>>> 32 Vassar St. G 498

messages.subject: Laundry Room Flooding
messages.body:
Hi,

I’ve tried using the laundry room in the basement 4 times and it’s been completely flooded every time. I submitted a maintenance request before that was closed, but this is becoming unusable - I’ve been having to go to a laundromat despite choosing this apartment largely for the in-building washing. Please let me know if there is another place I can submit a request.

Thanks,
Karthik

threads.subject: Re: [tickets.cs.columbia.edu #200010] Re: [Phd-students] Fall 2025 Registration OPEN - PhD Funding & Insurance Deadlines
threads.body:
Hi Da'Shante,

I appreciate the information, and apologize for the delay in my response.
Thanks for adding the courses that carry over, and I'll submit the import
requests this week.

For the courses I have remaining left to take, I wanted to confirm that
COMS 9902 Generative AI: Technical and Social taught by Eugene Wu would
count as an elective credit; I am enrolled in the class because it is
directly related to my PhD research, but I wasn't sure what the 9902 stood
for and hence wanted to confirm.

I sincerely appreciate all your help and patience through the
registration/PhD process!

Thanks,
Karthik

messages.body:
Hi Da'Shante,

I hope you're doing well! I was wondering if we could schedule a meeting
tomorrow or early next week to chat - I did my master's at Columbia and was
a little unsure of how I am supposed to go about choosing classes for the
PhD. Would sincerely appreciate some guidance with this, appreciate your
help!

Thanks,
Karthik

messages.subject: [tickets.cs.columbia.edu #200010] Re: [Phd-students] Fall 2025 Registration OPEN - PhD Funding & Insurance Deadlines
messages.body:
Hello,

Thank you for your email. We will get back to you as soon as possible, but
please keep in mind this can be 1-2 business days during busy times.

Helpful Resources

PhD Program Requirements
<https://www.cs.columbia.edu/education/phd/requirements/ >

Milestones Registration Form
<https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdNaXIbsM7AEo0iJuDbf1impCQPYmXstwwUART6Qfxilvw7MA/viewform >

Finance Questions: student-finances@cs.columbia.edu

In an emergency call Public Safety at 212-854-2878

For Wellness issues:
- CC/SEAS undergraduate students should be referred to their CSA advisor
- SEAS graduate students should be referred to seas-wellness@columbia.edu
- GS undergraduate students should be referred to gshealthandwellness@columbia.edu
- Barnard undergraduate students should be referred to the CARES Non-Emergency Line: 212-854-3362

If you have experienced an incident on campus, including discrimination, please report it using this link: https://universitylife.columbia.edu/report 

If you want to follow up on this issue, please include the ticket ID
[phd-advising #200010] in the subject line of all future correspondence.

Thank you,
CS Student Services

messages.subject: [tickets.cs.columbia.edu #200010] Re: [Phd-students] Fall 2025 Registration OPEN - PhD Funding & Insurance Deadlines
messages.body:
Hi Karthik,

First, as discussed in Monday's onboarding session, you must register for RESI
GR "1 RESIDENCE UNIT MORNINGSIDE CAMPUS" which is Call Number 10051. If you do
not register for this, you are not considered a full-time student. It looks
like a course, but it is a zero-credit enrollment category. You will enroll in
it for four fall and spring semesters in order to be a full-time student.

Next, I've updated your GDCOMS Mice profile with the courses that carry over
from your MS. You will need to take a theory course and 3 more electives. I
see you have done two imports for your MS, but you will follow the PhD import
instructions we'll discuss on August 25th if you'd like to include those.

Please let me know if you have any other questions.

Best,
Da'Shante

On Thu Aug 07 22:58:21 2025, ks4190@columbia.edu wrote:

    Hi Da'Shante,
     
    I hope you're doing well! I was wondering if we could schedule a meeting
    tomorrow or early next week to chat - I did my master's at Columbia and
    was a little unsure of how I am supposed to go about choosing classes for
    the PhD. Would sincerely appreciate some guidance with this, appreciate
    your help!
     
    Thanks,
    Karthik
     
    On Thu, Aug 7, 2025 at 1:29 PM PhD Student Services <
    phd-advising@cs.columbia.edu> wrote:

        Hi everyone,
         
        As a reminder, fall 2025 registration is open. I am sending more
        personal registration reminders to smaller groups of students and
        individuals as I review fall 2025 registration. Please keep an eye on
        your email and do the required action as soon as possible.
         
        The Student Health Insurance plan is set to expire on August 14, 2025
        if you are not enrolled for fall 2025. You will also not appear on the
        Finance Team's fall 2025 hiring/funding roster! They cannot complete
        their tasks if you are not enrolled in fall 2025.
         
        Thank you,
        Da'Shante
         
        Da'Shante Smith
        Associate Director of Academic Administration and Student Services
        Computer Science | Fu Foundation School of Engineering and Applied
        Science

threads.originalBody:
Dear Tenants,

This is a general reminder to all tenants that rent is due on
the 1st of each month. We kindly ask you to ensure that your rent
payment is submitted on time. Timely payments are essential for maintaining
the smooth operation of the building and ensuring all services and
facilities continue to function effectively.

Please also remember to double-check that your Water/Sewer Fee is paid in
full alongside your rent.
If payment is not received by the 5th of the month, a late fee will be
applied to your account. To avoid this fee, we strongly encourage you to
submit your payment today or tomorrow if you have not already done so.

Your prompt attention to this matter is greatly appreciated.
Thank you for fulfilling this important responsibility.

Best Regards,

MD Squared Property Group

threads.subject: Re: Missing Final Official Transcripts - Action Required
threads.body:
Hi,

I'm a little confused by this. I did my master's at Columbia itself - do
you need me to request transcripts from the university then send them back
to the university? I also had submitted all my undergraduate transcripts
before I enrolled in the MS program - is it possible to transfer those
over, or do I need to redo this?

Thanks,
Karthik
threads.originalBody:
You will receive a hold.

Office of Graduate Admissions | Columbia Engineering

                        Office of Graduate Admissions                      

Dear Karthik,

                      You are receiving this email because it appears that we have not received all of your final official transcripts and/or proof of degree conferral.

                      For clarification, official and final transcript must have:                      	                           Confirmation that your degree was conferred or awarded, with the date                        
	                           Final grades for any class listed as in-progress during the admissions process                        

                      ​​
Graduating soon? You should not request your transcript until your degree is conferred.

                      The deadline to submit final official transcripts is Tuesday, October 15, 2025. You may review all outstanding items via your Enrollment Checklist <https://apply.engineering.columbia.edu/apply/status > and below.

If we do not receive your final official transcripts, a registration hold will be placed on your account for the Spring semester.

                      As a friendly reminder, we require certified and secure online official transcripts that are submitted directly to Columbia Engineering from your prior academic institution(s)' Registrar Office.

                      We are missing the following item(s) from you: 
                      Official Transcript for Columbia University
Official Transcript for University Of California, Berkeley
Official Transcript for University of California: Berkeley
Official Transcript for University of California: Berkeley

                      Please send your transcript electronically to finaldocs@columbia.edu | Columbia University, Fu Foundation School of Engineering and Applied Science. Transcripts sent to other offices at Columbia cannot be retrieved. Read more on Columbia Engineering's Official Transcript policy <https://www.gradengineering.columbia.edu/faq/admitted-students > on our website.

                      If you have questions about how to submit final official transcripts, please refer to our FAQ <https://www.gradengineering.columbia.edu/faq/admitted-students >. If you believe you have received this email in error, please email finaldocs@columbia.edu. 

                      Thank you,
                       
                      Office of Graduate Admissions
                      The Fu Foundation School of Engineering and Applied Science
                      Columbia University
seasgradmit@columbia.edu

threads.subject: Re: EYP 2025 – Monday Recap + Tuesday Schedule
threads.body:
Hi,
I woke up under the weather today and just admittedly got up enough to get on my computer - I’m not sure I’ll be able to make it to the remaining sessions today either.
I’ll make sure to check in with a classmate regarding content I miss - but please let me know if there’s a way I can make up my attendance for today if needed. Sincerely apologize for the inconvenience but hope that you can understand.
Thanks,
Karthik
threads.originalBody:
Dear Karthik ,

Thank you for joining us today for the first day of *Engineering Your Ph.D.
(EYP) 2025*! We hope the sessions gave you a strong start to your doctoral
journey and introduced you to the people and resources that will support
you along the way.

As promised, attached are PDF versions of today’s presentations for your
reference.

*Tomorrow’s Schedule – Tuesday, August 26*

*Location: Carleton Commons (Mudd, 4th floor)*

   - 11:00 – 11:15 AM – Welcome and Brief Overview
   - 11:15 – 12:15 PM – CTL: Essentials of Teaching
   - 12:15 – 1:00 PM – Lunch
   - 1:00 – 2:00 PM – SEAS TA Panel
   - 2:00 – 3:00 PM – Inclusion and Belonging

*Looking Ahead to Thursday*

   -

   We will close EYP with a *Toast Reception* on *Thursday, August 28, from
   1:00 – 3:00 PM in Carleton Commons, *a chance to gather together and
   celebrate the start of your Ph.D. journey.
   -

   In addition, we are planning a *Thursday evening social event*. More
   details will follow soon. Stay tuned!

We look forward to seeing you back tomorrow as we continue with sessions on
teaching, the TA experience, and building a sense of belonging within the
Columbia Engineering community.

Best regards,
*Richard Hagen, Ph.D.* *(He/Him)*
Assistant Director, Graduate Student Affairs
Engineering Student Affairs
Columbia Engineering

--

threads.subject: Re: AI Contest Payment
threads.body:
Hi Julia,

I received the payment yesterday, thanks for all your help!

Thanks,
Karthik
threads.originalBody:
Hi Karthik!

My name is Julia and I'm the program coordinator at the Brown Institute.
Congrats on being selected as a winner for your project, "Making newsrooms’
institutional memory easily searchable by enabling conversational access!"
I'll be helping to facilitate your payment of $3,750 for your award. To
start, I'll send you an invitation to set up a Columbia vendor profile on a
platform called PaymentWorks. Could you please confirm:

1. Your full legal name (as written on tax documents)
2. A non-Columbia email I can use for the account

Thanks!
Julia

messages.subject: Re: AI Contest Payment
messages.body:
Hi Karthik,

Thanks for checkin in. I'm not sure if you received this email but I just
noticed that your vendor profile was returned, asking you to make some
changes, noted below:

*Hello and thank you for your submission, This message is to request the
following adjustment(s) be made to your submission: 1. Under " Tax
Information", under “Country of Citizenship", please revise your selection
2. The submitted W9 contains editable fields. This is not allowable by the
IRS. Please print to pdf so the fields will be locked. Once corrected,
please re-sign and re-upload. As opposed to uploading a manual copy of the
W9, a more-expedient option is to select "Generate Electronic W9" on the
registration to let PaymentWorks build your compliant W9 automatically from
the data you have already supplied. 3. Under "Additional Information",
under "Are you a US Citizen or US Legal Resident?", select US Legal
Resident. Once changes have been made and all applicable attachments have
been uploaded, please resubmit.*

You can log in here: https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.paymentworks.com_df_accounts_login_&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=ul2C4gSKnDdPyfogLxXv4C4EC3r3bSr-1MHP5lEkr2qZXkVEQUrOd8yiT8fTSK__&s=jX4_o47gV43PBR58PBVLkzR-0DDmED1z-S-4hZRv4Xk&e= 
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.paymentworks.com_df_accounts_login_&d=DwQCaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=JVjzU-49dWsHf6wqfVVCC1opArz0lLoTe5YhYhDATvk&m=DVw07UdD69qZSAqNSGFalmhTKN3LhlNPfTX9IRKNBOjcz6aycvV_qDXx1v6Od7YG&s=9BiNPqNfPje05aDj_I_6vUIby51E8N-lY43W-ckBSLE&e=>

Let me know if you have any questions.
Best,
Julia

messages.subject: Re: AI Contest Payment
messages.body:
Hi Julia,

Thanks for following up, I did just see this! I will be sure to correct the
issues today. However, I wanted to ask - in the original
notification email, it said there would be another email with next
steps/requirements for the grant - I don't think I've located that, wanted
to check in to see if you had any additional information on who the email
would have been from, the subject, etc.

Thanks so much for your help throughout this process.

Thanks,
Karthik

mh3287@columbia.edu.

I hope this is helpful!
Best,
Julia

threads.subject: Re: FWK - json results from last night game
threads.body:
Hi,

Thanks for sharing, I got a little unexpectedly busy this week, but will
work on a script by the end of the week and let you know as I progress/if I
have any other questions!

Thanks,
Karthik
threads.originalBody:
The below is the latest prompt template

Generate a single HTML report of player charts from this JSON file.

General formatting

- Title = player name once (no initials).

- Everyone starts at 1000 and show the start time under the x-axis at the
first hand.

- Mark highest (black), lowest (red), and the final score (black if ≥1000,
red if <1000).

- At the highest point also add caption (small, left-aligned,
word-per-line):
The
Most
#
For
The
night

- At the lowest point add caption (small, left-aligned, word-per-line, in
red):

The
Lowest
#
For
The
night

- X-axis = EST with ticks every 20 minutes.

- Y-axis = ‘Points at end of every hand’ from 350 to 1450 in steps of 100.

- Remove any “Matplotlib Chart” text.

- Output a single self-contained HTML file (charts embedded as base64 PNG).

- One section per player, sorted alphabetically.

Event annotations (smaller fonts, stacked, left-aligned, no overlaps)

- Use small, non-bold font for all annotations (game names, Scoops, Quads,
Straight Flushes, deltas).

- Game names must be stacked one word per line and left-aligned.

- Placement rules:

   • Wins (Δ > 50) → place above the line.

   • Losses (Δ < -50) → place below the line.

- Always avoid overlapping labels: stagger left/right, jitter vertically,
and use angled arrows if crowded.

- Show delta under the game name, stacked: e.g.

Fifty
Three
2
Rows
+93
Scoops - Mark scoops as stacked label:
Scoop
+<points>
HH:M
- Prefer below the line if space, otherwise above.
- Include the exact EST time.

Quads

- For Quads, show the actual rank repeated four times, stacked, with points
and time, e.g.:

   KKKK

   +36

   23:28

- Parse from hand data, not just defaulting to AAAA.

Straight Flush
- Show as stacked, e.g.:
SF to Q
+72
22:14

- If top card < Q, just say “Straight Flush”.

- Always include points and time.

Implementation details

- Never overlap labels or arrows; if space is tight, angle arrows outward
and push labels left/right.

- All labels must remain readable and uncluttered.

threads.subject: Re: random feature request for gmail twin
threads.body: Sounds good ! I spent today working on letting the user put in categories they want , tomorrow morning before our meeting I’ll try some more to see if I can automate it, will keep this in mind!
threads.originalBody:
I recently submitted a grant for TRI with Andrew Head. Of course, we
waiting until the last minute. Most of our history of collaboration is in
my inbox.

I'd love to be able to 1) find all the emails related to the grant and 2)
analyze them to see how and went we actually did the work... so for next
time I could have a better estimate of the time it takes and our cadence
for working.

Not necessarily an immediate thing, just a goal to keep on the list :)

a doc)

soon. Maybe not now… but definitely once we get gpt 5 :)

threads.subject: Re: Fw: Resident Memo – Community Guidelines & Building Security
threads.body: yea i get all these thanks though!
threads.originalBody:
Dear Residents,

Management's  committed  to maintaining a safe and comfortable living
environment. Residents are urged to follow these important guidelines:

   - *Be mindful of noise levels*—please refrain from playing loud music or
   TV between 9:30 PM and 9:00 AM
   - *Clean up after yourself *in all shared spaces, including hallways,
   staircases, and laundry rooms.
   - *Dispose of garbage and recycling properly*—please use designated bins
   and avoid leaving trash in hallways or common areas.
   - *Smoking is strictly prohibited anywhere on the premises.* Violations
   will result in a $250 fine.
   - *Do not buzz in anyone you do not personally know.* Unauthorized entry
   compromises the safety of the entire building.
   - *Ensure the front door fully closes and locks behind you* when
   entering or exiting the building.
   - *If you notice someone inside the building who appears unauthorized*,
   report it to management immediately.
   - *Package safety:* If you are expecting a delivery, retrieve it as soon
   as possible to help prevent theft. If your building has a designated
   package room, please ensure the door is securely closed after use.

We appreciate your cooperation in keeping our community safe and secure.
If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to reach out.

Best Regards,
MD Squared Property Group

 Dear Residents,

Management's  committed  to maintaining a safe and comfortable living environment. Residents are urged to follow these important guidelines:

   - Be mindful of noise levels—please refrain from playing loud music or TV between 9:30 PM and 9:00 AM
   - Clean up after yourself in all shared spaces, including hallways, staircases, and laundry rooms.
   - Dispose of garbage and recycling properly—please use designated bins and avoid leaving trash in hallways or common areas.
   - Smoking is strictly prohibited anywhere on the premises. Violations will result in a $250 fine.
   - Do not buzz in anyone you do not personally know. Unauthorized entry compromises the safety of the entire building.
   - Ensure the front door fully closes and locks behind you when entering or exiting the building.

We appreciate your cooperation in keeping our community safe and secure.  If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to reach out.

Folks:

This morning I'll be referring to the syllabus from my own section of CC.
Please see attached.

Also, we will look at some examples of CC midterm questions. Here's one
below.

Best

Matt

Identify the following text -- where it comes from and the significance of
the passage within its argumentative context. Your answer should consist of
one shortish paragraph (equivalent to no more than one side of one page in
the blue book). Identify the passage by number. (13 pts.; 3 for
identification, 10 for significance.)

“Every art and every inquiry, and similarly every action and pursuit, is
thought to aim at some good; and for this reason the good has rightly been
declared to be that at which all things aim. But a certain difference is
found among ends; some are activities, others are products apart from the
activities that produce them. Where there are ends apart from the actions,
it is the nature of the products to be better than the activities.”

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Matthew Connelly

messages.subject: Re: syllabus, and something we will be looking at during our first session
messages.body:
Dear Friends:

Lydia and I are looking forward to seeing you in the Studio this morning.
Please note that, based on our experience last time, we decided to reverse
the topic we will be exploring today. Instead of text and context, we are
going to focus on "Finding Connections." I've pasted the relevant part of
the syllabus below.

Best

Matt

Session 3: Finding Connections (early- to mid-November 2025)

Making meaningful connections between texts is one of the most powerful
ways to deepen understanding and strengthen memory. Drawing links—across
works, authors, disciplines, and traditions—can help us grasp abstract
ideas more concretely, see recurring patterns more clearly, and appreciate
nuance more fully. But our brains often miss these opportunities. Sometimes
we forget what we've read, or focus too narrowly. Sometimes connections
only emerge after repeated encounters or guided reflection.

In this session, students will experiment with using AI to surface
connections between core texts—primarily from Contemporary Civilization,
but also from Literature Humanities, Art Humanities, and Music Humanities.
They will try prompting AI to relate different texts by theme (e.g.,
justice), by intellectual lineage (e.g., Plato and Augustine), by
rhetorical style (e.g., prophetic, dialogic, polemical), or by formal or
aesthetic strategies.

Faculty will support students in critically evaluating AI-generated
connections: Are they substantive or superficial? Surprising or strained?
What interpretive assumptions are embedded in the link? Students will then
practice refining their own prompts to surface more meaningful insights and
reflect on how the process enhances or limits their own capacity for
synthesis. Ultimately, this session asks: What does it mean to read in
conversation—not just with a single author, but with a tradition? And how
can AI, used wisely, help us become more attentive and imaginative readers?

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Matthew Connelly

Professor of History

Vice Dean for AI Initiatives

403 Fayerweather Hall

Department of History

Mail Code 2508

1180 Amsterdam Avenue

Columbia University

New York, NY 10027

(212) 854-4563

threads.subject: Compensation and Stipend Increases

messages.subject: Compensation and Stipend Increases
messages.body:
Dear Student,

Please see the below October 6, 2025, communication from the University
regarding increases to student support.  Depending upon whether you receive
wages or stipend, your increase will be in your paycheck or your student
account.

Should you have any questions regarding your payment, please follow you
with your academic department.

Best regards,
SEAS Grad Funding

*From: *Office of Public Affairs <columbianews@m.columbia.edu>
*Reply-To: *Office of Public Affairs <
reply-fec715767d61077c-1318_HTML-57385648-546002910-3003@m.columbia.edu>
*Date: *Monday, October 6, 2025 at 9:46 PM
*Subject: *Compensation and Stipend Increases

Columbia will implement the following increases to student support, applied
pro rata effective October 16, 2025.

[image: Columbia University logo]

[image: A view looking over Alma Mater’s shoulders across campus to Butler
Library.]

*October 6, 2025*

Dear Students and Faculty,

As you are aware, the University and the Student Workers of Columbia union
(SWC) have made little progress in negotiating a successor contract since
our first and only bargaining session in March. Information on our most
recent bargaining attempt
<https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=341c47e64254658159304d035a980065daca9c4bba43f394e39c4e741337bf5855b8ff4fb976d0f34fb6b8ae5b7f485fdce22f04b1ed8eb3c51574ae4cac4532 >
is available. Still, the University remains fully committed to supporting
our graduate student community and student employees.

Columbia has some of the finest young scholars in the world, who are
focused on their coursework, teaching, and research, and who contribute
much to making Columbia the great institution that it is. Accordingly,
although the University continues to navigate difficult financial headwinds
that will persist for the foreseeable future, Columbia *will implement the
following increases to student support, applied pro rata effective October
16, 2025*:

   - *Compensation will increase by 3%* for PhD student employees who hold
   a teaching or research appointment in a guaranteed year of support in GSAS,
   SEAS, the VP&S Coordinated Doctoral Programs in Biomedical Sciences, the
   School of Nursing, the Columbia Business School, the Mailman School of
   Public Health, GSAPP, Journalism, SIPA, and the School of Social Work. This
   increase will apply to the pay period beginning October 16, which is paid
   on November 7. PhD students receiving stipends at an on-appointment rate,
   including those beyond their guaranteed years of funding who have been
   released from teaching duties, will also receive 3% increases. Stipends
   payments will be issued the week of October 27, reflecting the increase for
   fall, prorated from the October 16 effective date.

   - PhD students not on appointment in guaranteed years of funding *will
   also receive 3% increases* to their minimum support stipends. Stipends
   payments will be issued the week of October 27, reflecting the increase for
   fall, prorated from the October 16 effective date.

   - In each academic unit, compensation * will increase by 3%* for
   undergraduate students, Master’s students, non-PhD doctoral students, and
   PhD students beyond their guaranteed years of support who hold appointments
   as Teaching Assistants, Teaching Fellows, Preceptors, Readers, Teaching
   Assistants III, Graduate Research Assistants and Department Research
   Assistants. This increase will apply to the pay period beginning October
   16, which is paid on November 7.

   - The University will also *increase the minimum hourly rate* for
   student employees who perform instructional or research work from
   $22.50/hour to $23.00/hour, starting October 20, 2025.

With the academic year now well underway, the University is disappointed
that the union has not returned to the bargaining table or responded to the
University’s proposals. Most recently, after canceling an agreed-upon
bargaining session on September 11, without explanation, SWC refused to
bargain at a scheduled meeting today, October 6. The union has posted that
the University “canceled” today’s session. The University’s bargaining team
was present at the neutral Interchurch location from the agreed-upon start
time and remained ready to bargain. SWC’s delegation arrived an hour late
to present demands but would not stay to negotiate or discuss scheduling
future sessions, asking instead for email replies. The full update on the
scheduled October 6 bargaining session
<https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=341c47e6425465814316b759a22f869e97d1b139590debad1e8ebc5f35410a188bb4da170daae4caa9f162fb6214bce74f3b305ba8c5daf70a35bd3a8b123f33 >
is available.

Additional information regarding negotiations is available under the Current
Negotiations
<https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=341c47e6425465819237c7b8c46a9e99e2d0d51f24b4c38df11640c6adce8c9c2cdec81af891f1970c10e3f9309fc9b9525c7c4616b44f4e3735eba1596596a5 >
tab on the Student Benefits website.

Thank you for everything you do to support our community and advance
Columbia’s teaching and research mission.

Sincerely,

Amy Hungerford
Dean and Executive Vice President, Faculty of Arts and Sciences

Cas Holloway
Special Advisor to Acting President Claire Shipman

*Copyright © 2025 Columbia University. All rights reserved.*

You are receiving this email because you are part of the Columbia
University community.

*Columbia University - Office of Public Affairs*
402 Low Library, MC 4321, 535 W. 116th St
New York, NY 10027

*Confidentiality Notice:*

*The information contained in the email message is intended only for the
personal and confidential use of the recipient(s) named above.  If the
reader of this message is not the intended recipient or an agent
responsible for delivering it to the intended recipient, you are hereby
notified that you have received this document in error and that any review,
dissemination, or copying of this message is strictly prohibited.  If you
have received this communication in error, please notify me immediately by
email, and delete the original message.*

messages.body:
Dear residents, NYC is expecting inclement weather tomorrow, October 30, with rainfall anticipated to reach up to one inch and wind gusts up to 20-30mph. In preparation for the weather, and to ensure everyone’s safety, please remember to keep

Dear residents, 

NYC is expecting inclement weather tomorrow, October 30, with rainfall anticipated to reach up to one inch and wind gusts up to 20-30mph. 

In preparation for the weather, and to ensure everyone’s safety, please remember to keep all windows shut, and move any outdoor furniture indoors and off of terraces, patios, and roof areas.  

If you experience any issues, please don't hesitate to reach out to your management team. 

Please also note the emergency line that can be reached anytime at (212) 518- 2690 ext 111. Our support center is also available at all times at support@md2pg.com. 

Thank you for your attention, enjoy your weekend, and please let us know if you have any questions or need anything.

Best regards,
MD2 Property Group

threads.subject: Thank you for your Application!

messages.subject: Thank you for your Application!
messages.body:
Hi, Karthik, 

Thank you for applying to the Research Intern - AI Agents (Job number:1903410) position at Microsoft!  We're glad you're interested in a career at Microsoft and we're here to help you find a perfect fit.
 
You may not receive feedback from us on your application directly, but please know that it’s being evaluated, and you’ll hear from us as soon as the review process is complete. If you’re selected for an interview, you’ll be notified by someone on the recruiting team.
 
You can view your application status updates through your Action Center. If you see the job moved to an Archived state, that means the position is either no longer open, you withdrew from consideration, or you were not selected for the role.
 
You may notice that we move your application from one role to another. This may happen a few times and is a normal part of our recruiting process. So, if you see the job you applied to in an Archived state, and a new job listed as Active, please know that this is normal and does not negatively impact your candidacy in any way.
 
How’s your profile?
A key part of the review process is evaluating your profile in relation to the job requirements, so please make sure your profile is accurate and extensive – it’s our first step in getting to know you! You can build your profile anyway you’d like – you can import it from LinkedIn, manually update it, or import/attach a resume. The most important thing is that your profile tells your story!
 
We encourage you to check back frequently and continue to look for opportunities that match your interests, as new jobs are being posted regularly.
 
Thank you,
Microsoft Recruiting

This mail is sent from an unmonitored mailbox. Please do not reply. 
Microsoft respects your privacy. To learn more, please read our Microsoft Data Privacy Notice.
