threads.subject: Happy Halloweek 🎃🕸️👻 | EGSC Weekly Newsletter

messages.subject: Happy Halloweek 🎃🕸️👻 | EGSC Weekly Newsletter
messages.body:
<https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/  >
*Hey Grad SEAS!*
Spooky season is here, and we’re celebrating this *Friday *at our* EGSC
Halloween Social*! Join us for festive *treats*, spooky *activities*, and
hauntingly *good vibes* with your fellow grad students 🎃👻

Don't forget to *follow us on Instagram @columbiaegsc
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.instagram.com/columbiaegsc/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg9getv3k$ > *to stay updated on all our
events!

*👻 Halloween Social 🎃*
 Join us for a spook-tacular afternoon of fall festivities!
📆 *Date: *October 31
⏰ *Time: *12:00 PM – 3:00 PM
📍 *Location:* Back of Carleton Commons
Come build a haunted Lego masterpiece, decorate your own pumpkin, and enjoy
cozy treats like hot cider, hot chocolate, and pie crust cookies.
Take a break from debugging, and start *de-BOO-gging* instead!! 🕸️🧡
Join us at our *weekly GBMs*, whether you are a Department Representative
or not! We meet on Fridays from 4-5 pm in Mudd 524.

This week, we will be joined by *CPS* and *Stan Sakry, Senior Director of
Facilities and Space Planning*!

*Lunch & Learn: LGBTQ+ History Month Edition*
Come grab lunch and celebrate LGBTQ+ History Month with us! While you eat,
enjoy a fun slideshow of surprising facts, trailblazing figures, and
milestones from LGBTQ+ history!

*Join the ELC Executive Board 2026!*
The Entrepreneurship Leadership & Consulting (ELC) Club is one of Columbia
Engineering’s oldest and most dynamic student organizations—connecting
aspiring entrepreneurs and future consultants through hands-on events,
speaker series, and startup collaborations.

They're looking for passionate students to join our 2026 Executive Board!
Open positions include VP Entrepreneurship, VP Management Consulting, VP
Tech Consulting, VP Operations, and VP External Affairs.

📅 Deadline: *October 31st, 11:59 PM*
🔗 Apply Here
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://forms.gle/xrtSTVBt23YSbwby7__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!XlQ6LxSGX7Asj52kkRQ_41EAnyPCGA9MeJiJeWIarXme4l0cxxsMQUAXB5EFTeuSzUEw6cb9ZQ7ZOp9jz4CTqfVTAkh6Tx2LSAKquFQ$>
Come join the *Columbia Entrepreneurship & Venture Society* for their *General
Body Meeting* this *Wednesday* at *11:00 a.m.* in *Uris 326*! They'll be
discussing upcoming events and ways to get involved in the club. Whether
you’re a returning member or new to CEVS, they'd love to see you there.
Meetings are held *every other Wednesday*, so don’t miss out on the chance
to connect, share ideas, and grow your network!

*The First-Ever Columbia Product Hackathon 2025 is here!* 🚀

Hosted by the *Columbia Product Managers Club (PMC)* in collaboration with
the *NYU Product Management Club (NYU PMC)* — and joined by *Wharton,
Cornell, Duke, Northeastern University, Boston University, Carnegie Mellon
University (CMU), University of Southern California (USC), and UC Berkeley* —
this event brings together the brightest product minds from top schools for
an epic, cross-campus innovation challenge.

Over *48 hours*, teams will *think, build, and pitch* product solutions to
real-world problems.

📅 *Event Timeline*
💻 *Nov 3:* Online Kickoff & Prompt Release
🏫 *Nov 4:* In-Classroom Round @ Columbia (Mudd 1127) at *6 PM*
🏁 *Nov 6:* National Semi-Finals @ NYU Campus
🏆 *Nov 9:* National Finals @ NYU Campus

🕓 *Application Deadline: Oct 29, 2025 (EOD)* — don’t miss your shot to
represent Columbia!

💰
*$3,000+ Prize Pool🎯 Mentorship *from top PM leaders in tech & consulting
*📜 Certificates, swag, and national recognition at NYU*

📝 *Sign up now — we’ll share more details after you register!*
Google Form Link:
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdOB91NpIxAMv0nuJbi6CjF-mvPQ1GPKI8rcYBtFbJ6MvDzYA/viewform__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCgAShtI5Q$ 
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdOB91NpIxAMv0nuJbi6CjF-mvPQ1GPKI8rcYBtFbJ6MvDzYA/viewform__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!VQBWTHLuV9S-vvKJg04JMYFEtMFDjEwDLWHH5dnXQpYDFZ1Y3qJ7GAUsU1KhqNBknk2U4G5JzcXoEZmfKokT1B4nfEfsXCfxorkgZ2Y$>
or scan the *QR code* on the poster.

📩 More details and team confirmations will be shared via email after
registration.
⚠️ *Registrations close October 29, EOD*.

💡 Let’s make history, Lions — *build, innovate, and lead the future of
product!* 🦁🔥

*Upcoming Columbia Business School Events!*

   - Weds, Nov 5, 6-7pm. Lang Talk with Cyrus Massoumi, founder of
Zocdoc. *RSVP
   here. <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/ia6dvjbz__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCgv2bokeg$ >*
   - Weds, Nov 12, 6-8:30pm. CBS Big Idea Pitch Competition and Networking
   event. *RSVP link pending.*
   - Thurs, Nov 13, 12:30-2:30pm. CBS Tabling: Taste & Shop at CBS. *RSVP
   here. <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/nvrzzvpv__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCgN9vDTCc$ >*
   - Fri, Nov 14, 10-1:30pm*. *The AI Advantage: Building and Funding the
   Next Wave of Startups. *RSVP here. <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/7nwiifge__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCgCzFTlUM$ >*

*The  <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_0jNg3xNYa3hzWLA__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg8ZaklaA$ >**Spring
2026 CBL Engineering application
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_0jNg3xNYa3hzWLA__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg8ZaklaA$ >** is now open!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_0jNg3xNYa3hzWLA__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg8ZaklaA$ >*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_0jNg3xNYa3hzWLA__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg8ZaklaA$ >
*Columbia Build Lab (CBL)
<https://business.columbia.edu/lang/student-and-alumni-programming/launch/columbia-build-lab  >*
matches
Columbia students pursuing Computer Science (or related degrees) with CBS
student-founders of an early-stage startup. Engineers in the Build Lab
program gain experience working to solve real-world challenges with teams
of peers and CBS founders. This is an excellent opportunity for any
engineering or computer science student looking to gain valuable experience
over the summer!
Submit your application to the Spring 2026 cohort by December 7!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_0jNg3xNYa3hzWLA__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCg8ZaklaA$ >
Interested in joining *Columbia’s only student club dedicated to
Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCI)*? At *Neurotech X Columbia*, you’ll find
opportunities to engage in cutting-edge research projects  and connect with
peers passionate about neurotechnology. We welcome all members of the
Columbia community to join us — please register through this Google Form:
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/u/1/d/1r6jLNyCo6pbxXR3jEqPT0a_FzcpqlB4d4iCH3_75ZbQ__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCgSbHABoA$ 
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/u/1/d/1r6jLNyCo6pbxXR3jEqPT0a_FzcpqlB4d4iCH3_75ZbQ__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!U66AXcO3CK09cjy5CEinktcs0lyktt1ow3JxvVucDXBZcW_LeYTqdaFHhq0nJeCzhWoF8A1KI_e8L7oaewiVfhUko8lKWg$>
[image: Instagram] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://instagram.com/columbiaegsc__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Q4RK2ytB-rX95NUgWHn_3W00ooMQkG8nWF2QO-yP3qL7YODEjMJS_HLLStgvgyyn59GGiKdwYdIZjav8rPwRhJBFrJLNlR6DEoeMNBbT6UCgIWl_f0k$ >

messages.subject: Thanks for applying to Google!
messages.body:
Hi Karthik Sreedhar,

Thanks for applying to Google! There are a ton of great companies out  
there, so
we appreciate your interest in joining our team.

We’ll review your application carefully and keep
Kavya Sreedhar, Abhijit Adsule updated on your progress. In the
meantime, learn more about our job opportunities and life at Google on our
YouTube channel: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.youtube.com/lifeatgoogle__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X72SYTBfRJfNUOHo9u7OPK_a7v4ml1fcoPqMzNCk7578UnJgFrqXv4oaAYGmyLqwY3TR0vDuIsI-jpxeXTSUww$ .

Thanks!

- The Google Recruiting Team

---
Google Inc. 1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043

related

threads.subject: Thanks for accepting Kavya Sreedhar’s invitation to apply to Google

messages.subject: Thanks for accepting Kavya Sreedhar’s invitation to apply to Google
messages.body:
Google Careers

Time to find the perfect role at Google

Hi Karthik Sreedhar,

We're thrilled that you've accepted Kavya's invitation to apply to Google!  
You have 30 days to apply to up to 3 jobs with this invitation, which also  
enables Kavya to receive updates on the status of your application(s). Make  
sure to apply using the invitation link and the same email address where  
you received the invitation. If you already applied within the last 30 days  
you don't have to apply again. However, your referrer may not receive  
updates on those applications. Remember, if you want to apply to additional  
roles once this invitation has expired, be sure to ask Kavya to send you a  
new invitation.

EXPLORE OPEN ROLES

Looking forward to seeing your application!

-The Google Staffing team

Info icon Need help finding the right role?

Kavya can always give you some pointers, but generally we recommend  
focusing first on roles where your skills match the minimum qualifications  
and some of the prefered qualifications, then tailoring your resume to  
highlight those skills. Learn more about how Google hires.

This email was sent to you as a transactional notification of activity  
related to Google Staffing.

Google logo
1600 Amphitheatre Parkway,
Mountain View, CA 94043, USA

You received this message because someone that works at Google referred you.

threads.subject: Invitation from Abhijit Adsule to apply to Google

messages.subject: Invitation from Abhijit Adsule to apply to Google
messages.body:
Google Careers logo

Build for everyone

Abhijit wants to work with you at Google

Hi KARTHIK SREEDHAR,

Abhijit has invited you to apply for a job at Google! Start the application  
process by viewing their invitation. By accepting their invitation, you'll  
allow us to share high level application status updates with this Googler.  
They will see the next 1-3 roles you apply to in the coming 30 days, as  
well as any roles you've applied to in the past 30 days where the  
application is still open. If you'd like Abhijit to be kept in the loop, be  
sure to accept and apply through the link using your ks4190@columbia.edu  
email address! If you want to later apply to more roles, make sure to ask  
them to send you a new invitation. You may also decline the invitation if  
you'd prefer that status updates not be shared with Abhijit.

The link to accept is only active for the next 30 days, so accept it soon.  
Then, follow the “Search jobs” link to learn more about open Google roles  
and apply. If you've applied to roles in the last 30 days, you don't have  
to apply again.

Make sure to use this invitation link so we know you've been referred.

VIEW INVITATION TO APPLY

We hope to hear from you soon,

The Google Staffing Team

Please note, any information you enter during the application and  
recruitment process will be kept confidential in compliance with Google's  
Candidate Privacy Policy.

Google logo

© 2021 Google LLC 1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043

You received this message because someone that works at Google referred you.

[image: unnamed.png]

Dear Computer Scientist,

Mercor Intelligence is hiring for a variety of projects to start this
winter and in 2026. We have part-time remote positions available in
computer science, AI, and data science. Our AI company assists major
artificial intelligence and social media companies in San Francisco and
Silicon Valley. We recruit computer scientists around the nation and around
the world. To learn more about our positions available now, click the links
below.

*AI Red Teamer - Adversarial AI Advanced Testing *$54-$100 hourly,
Part-time or Full-time, Remote
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://work.mercor.com/jobs/list_AAABmX4FiBxRn-I1VM5KeZ2h?referralCode=f529623d-0b53-4f0a-9f1d-2a0d884c641f__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RL2VlSsAzD0ZQb6OWGNlRdDvuF9fpMDQxPBcwo6VCvLRqTr4zE7r7mDPRRJ1yUzQbJxBiZmpL5a4883BcU8IbRpxU6TL9Wyb$ 

*Data Science, AI Tutor* $45-$100 hourly, Full-time, Remote or at office in
Palo Alto
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://work.mercor.com/jobs/list_AAABmfXLudLUdLZDSaZBN687?referralCode=f529623d-0b53-4f0a-9f1d-2a0d884c641f__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RL2VlSsAzD0ZQb6OWGNlRdDvuF9fpMDQxPBcwo6VCvLRqTr4zE7r7mDPRRJ1yUzQbJxBiZmpL5a4883BcU8IbRpxU8hBZNtS$ 

*Software Developer (Active Github Contributor) *$80-$110 hourly,
Part-Time, Remote
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://work.mercor.com/jobs/list_AAABmemAaWdDQBM_XjVDybH-?referralCode=f529623d-0b53-4f0a-9f1d-2a0d884c641f__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RL2VlSsAzD0ZQb6OWGNlRdDvuF9fpMDQxPBcwo6VCvLRqTr4zE7r7mDPRRJ1yUzQbJxBiZmpL5a4883BcU8IbRpxUyHRle_t$

Rebecca Brudzynski 😀

threads.subject: RE: Welcome to the Columbia University Student Health Insurance Plan

messages.subject: Welcome to the Columbia University Student Health Insurance Plan
messages.body:
Hi Karthik,

Thank you for your email and I hope you are doing well!

All full-time students are automatically enrolled unless they manually request to opt out and are approved for a waiver. Students need to submit a new waiver request annually for each plan year. The Fall enrollment period ran from July 15 –September 30, 2025.

If you have existing insurance coverage which meets the waiver criteria<https://www.health.columbia.edu/content/request-waiver > and missed the deadline, you may submit a Late Waiver Request. Please upload the following documents through your Patient Portal<https://cas.columbia.edu/cas/login?service=https%3a%2f%2fsecure.health.columbia.edu > for review:

  1.  The Request for Late Waiver Form – found in your Patient Portal<https://cas.columbia.edu/cas/login?service=https%3a%2f%2fsecure.health.columbia.edu > under Downloadable Forms on the left-hand side menu.

  1.  A copy of the front and back of your insurance card.

  1.  A Proof of Coverage Letter from your existing insurance company that states your name and the effective date of coverage. We are unable to accept email notices.

Once received, please allow typically up to 7-10 business days for processing. If approved, your Columbia insurance will be terminated as of the date of submission and your student account will be pro-rated and adjusted accordingly.

Warm Regards,

Giselle Roman

Health Insurance Representative
Columbia University Health
https://www.health.columbia.edu/ 

Confidentiality Disclaimer: This e-mail message and any attachments are private communication and may contain confidential, privileged information meant solely for the intended recipient. If you are not the intended recipient, you are hereby notified that any use, dissemination, distribution or copying of this communication is strictly prohibited. Please notify the sender immediately by replying to this message, then delete the e-mail and any attachments from your system. Thank you.

Sent: Thursday, October 23, 2025 3:19 PM
To: studentinsurance <studentinsurance@cumc.columbia.edu>
Subject: Re: Welcome to the Columbia University Student Health Insurance Plan

You don't often get email from ks4190@columbia.edu. Learn why this is important<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://aka.ms/LearnAboutSenderIdentification__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!UI98oX8hPizmKrF5qtdoUaHMP5pbQoIQdgNCNFA87y2ITMXlQw3w274HcDqTkhCGrZj7rPqNVgtF-hHz7jqStyKpOY_SJ2LiLksF11JF2A$ >
Hi,

I meant to opt out of the insurance plan as I have my own, wanted to reach out to see the steps to do so.

Thanks,
Karthik

Dear Student,
Welcome to the 2025-2026 Aetna Student Health insurance plan. This message confirms your enrollment in the plan.

Next Steps

Print Your Insurance ID Card<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/columbia.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7a3cd38b05cd33d61d2096783&id=c571a016e4&e=0faec86530__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QgzhHbrCqfkWV27cOaGgndhsUKSek42KBH8jkRrybB29I2ItXqNyXOvixhXPwv0vhXMpdCUP_WcEgDyzM6vpG0hHgSdQyeLR7efZDmRqyw$> using your UNI and Date of Birth. It may take up to 5 business days from today for your insurance card and pharmacy access to be available.

Aetna Member Site<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/columbia.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7a3cd38b05cd33d61d2096783&id=3d1a10a9f1&e=0faec86530__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QgzhHbrCqfkWV27cOaGgndhsUKSek42KBH8jkRrybB29I2ItXqNyXOvixhXPwv0vhXMpdCUP_WcEgDyzM6vpG0hHgSdQyeLR7eejyO49_w$> is your secure member website to access your benefits, health and claims information 24/7. You may obtain your Aetna ID by accessing your Insurance ID Card as instructed above.

If you are issued a referral <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/columbia.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7a3cd38b05cd33d61d2096783&id=2a903ccae6&e=0faec86530__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QgzhHbrCqfkWV27cOaGgndhsUKSek42KBH8jkRrybB29I2ItXqNyXOvixhXPwv0vhXMpdCUP_WcEgDyzM6vpG0hHgSdQyeLR7ecHoMHoKw$> from Columbia Health Medical Services or Counseling and Psychological Services for off-campus care, find a preferred provider online through Aetna DocFind<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/columbia.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7a3cd38b05cd33d61d2096783&id=f80a2a6188&e=0faec86530__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QgzhHbrCqfkWV27cOaGgndhsUKSek42KBH8jkRrybB29I2ItXqNyXOvixhXPwv0vhXMpdCUP_WcEgDyzM6vpG0hHgSdQyeLR7edLjq6jmQ$>.

IMPORTANT:

A referral from Columbia Health is required for most off-campus services.

All 2024-2025 policy year medical referrals expired on August 14, 2025 and must be renewed at Columbia Health Medical Services prior to continuing off campus medical care. Referrals issued for mental health services are valid as long as you remain continuously enrolled in the Columbia University Student Health Insurance Plan. If you are residing more than 50 miles away from campus referrals are not required.

Important Information About Your Plan

Detailed information about the Columbia Student Health Insurance Plan<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/columbia.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7a3cd38b05cd33d61d2096783&id=7324c2ee65&e=0faec86530__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QgzhHbrCqfkWV27cOaGgndhsUKSek42KBH8jkRrybB29I2ItXqNyXOvixhXPwv0vhXMpdCUP_WcEgDyzM6vpG0hHgSdQyeLR7efY-mGZ0Q$> including Referral Requirements<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/columbia.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7a3cd38b05cd33d61d2096783&id=b240cf9e0c&e=0faec86530__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QgzhHbrCqfkWV27cOaGgndhsUKSek42KBH8jkRrybB29I2ItXqNyXOvixhXPwv0vhXMpdCUP_WcEgDyzM6vpG0hHgSdQyeLR7edCzK0I2w$> for off-campus care.

For More Information
Contact Aetna Student Health customer service<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/columbia.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7a3cd38b05cd33d61d2096783&id=1ea98c9077&e=0faec86530__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QgzhHbrCqfkWV27cOaGgndhsUKSek42KBH8jkRrybB29I2ItXqNyXOvixhXPwv0vhXMpdCUP_WcEgDyzM6vpG0hHgSdQyeLR7efGguHIxg$> or visit the Aetna Member Site. You may also contact the Student Health Insurance Office via email studentinsurance@columbia.edu<mailto:studentinsurance@columbia.edu>.

Be Well,
Student Health Insurance Office

threads.subject: PhD Medical Campus Mixer!

messages.subject: PhD Medical Campus Mixer!
messages.body:
Hi PhD students!

Gigi here, the Engineering Graduate Student Council (EGSC) Interschool
Chair. You may or may not have been to my EGSC Cocoahosue events, or more
recently, Pitch Night with the Columbia Business School. My role in this
position is to integrate different schools/campuses for events. Currently,
I am a biomedical engineering PhD student working on the medical campus,
and have heard that many of you who work up there would like more
programming nearby. That being said, I am very excited to announce that *EGSC
is hosting our first-ever PhD mixer on the uptown medical campus! 💃*

We will be hanging out at *Fort Washington (3938 Broadway) *from
*5:30-7:30PM* with our fellow colleagues from the Graduate Student
Organization (GSO), which is the body that governs the VP&S Biomedical
Sciences PhD students (think neurology, nutrition, microbiology, the list
goes on!).

This event is intended for* PhD students who work on the medical
campus*. Eventbrite
tickets are accessible here
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.eventbrite.com/e/phd-uptown-mixer-tickets-1815621726459?aff=oddtdtcreator__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!W_OnMbBEAu1ibBGL8heEoMn_JXk27Oh6YmWkwIbJX6rF56eTUOp7d8gK3NsrWLr4eGd7UUknpcv4bw-fR1ngdp7GjNCMY6CRKzNei-a_1US5CjJSLcnSuRk$ >.
Food and drinks will be provided.

Hope to see you there!

-- 
____________________________________________________________

*Gigi Bond*
Inter-school Chair, Engineering Graduate Student Council
*Columbia Engineering*
interschool-chair@egsc.seas.columbia.edu

messages.subject: Cocoahouse this Thursday ☕️🍪| EGSC Weekly Newsletter
messages.body:
<https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/  >
*Hey Grad SEAS!*
Happy Tuesday! We hope you had a great time at our *October Carleton
Commons Social* last Friday 🍕🪩🕺💃!

This *Thursday*, we're hosting our *EGSC Cocoahouse*! See you there!

Don't forget to *follow us on Instagram @columbiaegsc
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.instagram.com/columbiaegsc/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!SVb6M51tAEHXSOqSq5Ut6fAuy5Ql_-GyB4z5jWOV4YxJbGk3BCb3D6bZx0YytbmBerdG5A6e296h6hqw3mmLU7BODytRV1bd04u-sHt54pyT0UkFV38$ > *to stay updated on all our
events!
*Caffeinate and conversate* with other students from graduate SEAS! Enjoy
coffee, cookies, and some great company *this Thursday* at *EGSC Cocoa
House*!
Join us at our *weekly GBMs*, whether you are a Department Representative
or not! We meet on Fridays from 4-5 pm in Mudd 524.

This week, we will be joined by *Stan Sakry, Senior Director of Facilities
and Space Planning*!

*Lunch & Learn: LGBTQ+ History Month Edition*
Come grab lunch and celebrate LGBTQ+ History Month with us! While you eat,
enjoy a fun slideshow of surprising facts, trailblazing figures, and
milestones from LGBTQ+ history!
Interested in joining *Columbia’s only student club dedicated to
Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCI)*? At *Neurotech X Columbia*, you’ll find
opportunities to engage in cutting-edge research projects  and connect with
peers passionate about neurotechnology. We welcome all members of the
Columbia community to join us — please register through this Google Form:
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/u/1/d/1r6jLNyCo6pbxXR3jEqPT0a_FzcpqlB4d4iCH3_75ZbQ__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!SVb6M51tAEHXSOqSq5Ut6fAuy5Ql_-GyB4z5jWOV4YxJbGk3BCb3D6bZx0YytbmBerdG5A6e296h6hqw3mmLU7BODytRV1bd04u-sHt54pyTV4bt_Qw$ 
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/u/1/d/1r6jLNyCo6pbxXR3jEqPT0a_FzcpqlB4d4iCH3_75ZbQ__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!U66AXcO3CK09cjy5CEinktcs0lyktt1ow3JxvVucDXBZcW_LeYTqdaFHhq0nJeCzhWoF8A1KI_e8L7oaewiVfhUko8lKWg$>

Tuesday, October 21, 2025 • 3 min read
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
Wake up with The Yodel
 |

| 
by Natasha Frost with Anthony Rivas
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
Wake up with The Yodel
 |

| 
by Natasha Frost with Anthony Rivas
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
Good morning, folks. The night sky will host a spectacular display tonight, with the Orionid meteor show coinciding with a new moon. Here’s how to see it. Now, on to the news.
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
|   |

 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
Need to know
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

| 
Kiichiro Sato/AP
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
A massive outage of Amazon’s cloud computing service disrupted almost every corner of the internet yesterday, including our ability to send this newsletter. Services were back to normal last night.
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|    
   - Background: Companies rely on cloud computing providers like Amazon to store their data and make software and applications run via data plants across the U.S., including one in Virginia, where the Domain Name System error occurred. [CBS News/Business Insider]   

   - Impacts: The outage prevented students from accessing their work, hungry diners from using delivery apps and crypto traders from using exchanges. It also disrupted Amazon-owned smart speakers, doorbell cameras and e-books. If it messed up your day too, here’s how to manage the next one. [AP]
   - Experts weigh in: The outage laid bare just how many global businesses rely on a small number of tech companies, analysts said. “We urgently need diversification in cloud computing,” one digital rights expert said. [The Guardian]
 |

| 
|   |

 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
In case you missed it
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

| 
Daniel Shirey/MLB Photos via Getty Images
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
Toronto triumphs in ALCS
 |

| 
In last night’s tiebreaking Game 7, the Blue Jays soared to a 4-3 win over the Mariners, thanks to a towering three-run homer by George Springer. Next, they’ll face the Dodgers in their first World Series appearance in 32 years, beginning Friday. Here’s what to expect. [Yahoo Sports]
 |

| 
Natl. Guard in Portland
 |

| 
An appellate court ruled that the Trump administration can deploy the National Guard in Portland, Ore., though another court order still blocks immediate action. Oregon officials vowed to appeal. Read the latest on President Trump’s efforts to federalize troops. [CNN]
 |

| 
Trump’s White House ballroom
 |

| 
Construction crews began tearing down part of the White House’s East Wing to make way for Trump’s $250 million ballroom, despite lacking federal approval. Trump previously said it was “privately funded” by “generous Patriots.” See pictures of the demolition. [AP]
 |

| 
A landmark blindness study
 |

| 
A small clinical study found that eight in 10 patients with severe macular degeneration experienced meaningful improvement in their vision after receiving a wireless eye implant and pairing it with augmented reality glasses — though it was only in black and white. [NBC News]
 |

| 
‘See you in Oz!’
 |

| 
Oscar nominee Colman Domingo will voice the grown-up Cowardly Lion in Wicked: For Good, after director Jon M. Chu reached out via Instagram DM. The sequel comes out Nov. 21. Watch the reveal, and see who’s part of the returning cast. [Yahoo Entertainment]
 |

| 
|   |

 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
What’s happening today
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

| 
James Manning/PA Images via Getty Images
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
In bookstores
 |

| 
Nobody’s Girl: A Memoir of Surviving Abuse and Fighting for Justice, the posthumous memoir by Virginia Giuffre, written with Amy Wallace, is out. The book details the alleged abuses by disgraced financier Jeffrey Epstein and Prince Andrew. Read a preview. [USA Today]
 |

| 
Now streaming
 |

| 
This one’s for baseball fans (Canadians especially): Who Killed the Montreal Expos?, which investigates who is to blame for the downfall of Canada’s first MLB team, is on Netflix. [Hollywood Reporter]
 |

| 
On the court
 |

| 
The 2025-26 NBA season tips off on NBC, with a 7:30 p.m. ET matchup between the Rockets and the Thunder, followed by a Warriors-Lakers faceoff at 10 p.m. ET. Here’s your guide to the full season. [AP/Yahoo Sports]
 |

| 
On TV
 |

| 
Wife Swap: The Real Housewives Edition premieres at 9 p.m. ET on Bravo. On tonight’s episode, Salt Lake City’s Angie Katsanevas slips out of her stilettos and into the life of former Mormon Lindsay Flake. [Us Weekly]
 |

| 
And don't forget to...
 |

| 
Read your daily horoscope. Play the crossword. Check the forecast in your area.
 |

| 
|   |

 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
Today in history
 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 |

 |

| 
| 
| 
In 1879, Thomas Edison invented the light bulb. Or, did he? The road to electric light was twistier than you might’ve heard. Although Edison and his team were the first to successfully test an incandescent light bulb, which burned for more than 13 hours, they built on the work of many others. Here’s how. [BGR]
 |

| 
Illustration: Yahoo News; photo: Bettmann Archive via Getty Images
 |

| 
|   |

 |

 |

 |

| 
| 
|  |

 | 
| 
Feel-good moment
 |

 |

 |

| 
|

messages.subject: ✨ Welcome to New York's biggest AI events calendar
messages.body:
✨ Welcome to New York's biggest AI events calendar
Hi there, Thank you for joining us at one of our co-hosted events! I'm happy to welcome you to the largest weekly newsletter dedicated to AI events GenerativeAINY.com At any given moment, almost 50 in-person AI events, from hackathons to conferences, workshops, and meetups, are scheduled in New York alone. Being on the list means you’ll receive updates on these events and other carefully curated AI gatherings across NY every Thursday at 7.30am ET. This calendar is not company, group, or host-specific, making it the most comprehensive calendar with AI events only. [ ✨ New] Join our growing communities: Become a Premium member to join a network of top-tier AI operators and founders and get free tickets to top conferences. Join the AI Judging community to judge world-class hackathons and strengthen your O-1/EB-1 visa petition. Partner with me to grow the biggest AI events community across the Bay Area & NY (can become a paid gig). Join a community - https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/genai-sf__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QoyO0__Fwpzq2lIg5BXsI_XKqkPoEpRDtBetXBDHFiFULbwXE0uBwThxUFC_hAl26luN3dCXOAsa3fnyqT0jtKViXm8ukYe1$  Not interested in these updates? No problem, simply click on the unsubscribe link below and you won't be contacted again. Otherwise, reply to this email with "Hey!" so the spam filter gods will spare future emails. Want to feature or highlight your event to 10k AI professionals? Sponsor an event? Reply to this email. Looking forward to seeing you at future events! Best, Sahar P.S. Are you Bay Area-bound or travel often? Check out the SF AI events calendar GenerativeAISF.com

threads.subject: Your monthly Columbia University statement

messages.subject: Your monthly Columbia University statement
messages.body:
Your monthly Columbia University statement

Student ID: C004741301

This is your monthly billing notification.  Your action may be needed.  You will have access to monthly statements for the remainder of the year through Student Services Online (SSOL).

Dear Karthik Sreedhar:

The monthly statement for your Columbia University account is now available on SSOL and will show an anticipated amount due or (credit) of $26.00.  Note: if the amount is in parentheses, it is an anticipated credit.If the amount shown is zero, no action is needed. If the amount shown is greater than zero make your payment by or before 11/06/2025. Balances that remain unpaid at the due date may incur late fees and will prevent students from registering, changing programs, or obtaining a diploma. We recognize these are extremely challenging and unique times. If you are a student experiencing financial hardship or are unable to register for an upcoming term as a result of account balances and need help financing your balance , please contact your school's financial aid office for assistance.Make your payment at SSOL at https://ssol.columbia.edu/ .  Please note: as of November 1, 2016, the University no longer accepts cash payments for student accounts. If you have arranged to receive funding from your academic department, that funding may not appear in your student account until the early registration period for the following term. If your funding does not appear by then, inquire with your department. If the amount shown is an anticipated credit -- indicated in parentheses as ($xx,xxx.xx) -- it may be the result of a financial aid award, a tuition deposit or overpayment of charges to your account. 

Please note: The amount shown is a snapshot of your account on the day the statement was produced.  This amount may not reflect any recent changes in your registration.  After you complete your registration, please review the recent activity in SSOL to see your most current amount due.

To avoid any impact to your registration, we encourage you to review your account regularly in SSOL.  If you have authorized a payer, they can also view your account by logging into the QuikPAY website at https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://quikpayasp.com/columbia/tuition/authorized.do__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!XBAKPMlNcIlDAHJEKIrykMy4X4oPhHGg68a-0SXF9C8tYXxzaF59MT9qKa4wylDWUAI1dwtz7BvVgiIvf6Kd5w$ .

You can also visit the Student Financial Services website for general information about:

 Billing and payments (http://sfs.columbia.edu/bills ) Other payment options and plans (http://sfs.columbia.edu/ways-to-pay)The  billing schedule (http://sfs.columbia.edu/bill-schedule)Late  payment fees (http://sfs.columbia.edu/unpaid-bills)Credit  balances and the automatic processing of refunds (https://sfs.columbia.edu/content/refunds ) 

If you have further questions, we encourage you to contact your campus service center below. 

Student Service Locations

For students at the Morningside Campus:

ssc@columbia.edu 

+1-212-854-4400

210 Kent Hall, Mail Code 9202

1140 Amsterdam Ave.

New York, NY 10027

For students at the Medical Center:

cumc-rfs@columbia.edu 

+1-212-342-4790

141 Black Building

154 Haven Avenue, Room 406  

New York, NY 10032

Sincerely,   

Student Financial Services

Columbia University

  Important Note about Privacy: Please remember that the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA) limits communications about a student account to the student and to those whom the student has expressly designated as authorized payers.

messages.subject: Invitation from Kavya Sreedhar to apply to Google
messages.body:
Google Careers logo

Build for everyone

Kavya wants to work with you at Google

Hi Karthik Sreedhar,

Kavya has invited you to apply for a job at Google! Start the application  
process by viewing their invitation. By accepting their invitation, you'll  
allow us to share high level application status updates with this Googler.  
They will see the next 1-3 roles you apply to in the coming 30 days, as  
well as any roles you've applied to in the past 30 days where the  
application is still open. If you'd like Kavya to be kept in the loop, be  
sure to accept and apply through the link using your ks4190@columbia.edu  
email address! If you want to later apply to more roles, make sure to ask  
them to send you a new invitation. You may also decline the invitation if  
you'd prefer that status updates not be shared with Kavya.

The link to accept is only active for the next 30 days, so accept it soon.  
Then, follow the “Search jobs” link to learn more about open Google roles  
and apply. If you've applied to roles in the last 30 days, you don't have  
to apply again.

Make sure to use this invitation link so we know you've been referred.

VIEW INVITATION TO APPLY

We hope to hear from you soon,

The Google Staffing Team

Please note, any information you enter during the application and  
recruitment process will be kept confidential in compliance with Google's  
Candidate Privacy Policy.

Google logo

© 2021 Google LLC 1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043

You received this message because someone that works at Google referred you.

Dear Karthik Sreedhar,

The Engineering School values your thoughtful feedback on your courses. Please take a few minutes to fill out the Course evaluations. These evaluations are extremely important for several reasons:

• They help the Engineering School and Departments evaluate instructor-effectiveness
• Instructors use the feedback to improve their courses for future students
• Your feedback helps fellow students choose their courses

The evaluation is available at:




Survey Summary

Course CodeCourse TitleSurvey Start DateSurvey End DateStatus
COMSE9902_048_2025_3-SEMINARINCOMPUTERSCIENCECOMSE9902_048_2025_3 - SEMINAR IN COMPUTER SCIENCE10/13/2025 12:00 AM10/20/2025 11:59 PMNot-SubmittedGo To Survey



This evaluation will remain open until 10/20/2025 11:59 PM. Please complete it before then.

Enter the site using your username and password. You may submit the evaluation once only.
If the above link is not working then please follow the Alternate Instructions at the bottom of the message.

Thank you for your time and attention in this important matter. Your feedback is very important.

Please note: Evaluations are confidential. Your instructors and TA's will never see your name associated with any evaluation responses.
------------------------------------------------------------
Should you encounter any technical difficulty in filling out the evaluation, please send an email to seas-learningsupport@columbia.edu clearly indicating the problem you encountered. For any other concerns please contact your department.

Alternate Instructions:

3) Go to your course
4) Click on 'Evaluation' link in the left navigation menu.

threads.subject: CCPE - 2026 Graduate Student Grant Opportunity

messages.subject: CCPE - 2026 Graduate Student Grant Opportunity
messages.body:
Dear SEAS Doctoral Students,

The *Columbia Center for Political Economy (CCPE)*
<http://politicaleconomy.columbia.edu/  > is now accepting applications for
its *2026 Graduate Student Grant Opportunity*.

*About the Grant*
These one-year awards support doctoral research that advances new knowledge
and fosters collaboration in the broad field of political economy. Research
areas include:

   - Firms and Industrial Policy
   - Work and Labor
   - Money and Finance
   - Climate
   - International Political Economy

*Funding*

   - Awards range from *$5,000–$15,000*
   - Larger awards may support collaborative or cross-departmental projects
   - In exceptional cases (with departmental approval), a semester-long
   teaching buyout may be funded

*Eligibility*
All *Columbia doctoral students* are eligible to apply. Cross-departmental
proposals are welcome.

*Deadline:* Monday, *October 20, 2025, at 5:00 p.m. ET*
*Award period:* January 1–December 31, 2026

*Apply Here!
<https://politicaleconomy.columbia.edu/news/now-open-2026-graduate-student-grant-opportunity  >*

Best,

*Graduate Student Affairs*

*Columbia Engineering*

threads.subject: REMINDER: Apply for February 11, 2026 MS Degree Conferral by November 1, 2025

messages.subject: REMINDER: Apply for February 11, 2026 MS Degree Conferral by November 1, 2025
messages.body:
Dear students,

If you are completing your MS degree requirements by the end of this Fall
semester, you should apply for the February 11, 2026 degree conferral. (
This includes MS-PhD students completing the MS portion of their program.)

The deadline to submit the degree application for the February 11, 2026 degree
conferral is Saturday, November 1, 2025. You must submit the University
Application for Degree form via SSOL
<https://registrar.columbia.edu/content/graduation-requirements-and-diplomas  >
by
this deadline in order to be considered for degree conferral. After the
Registrar’s Office processes your degree application, Engineering Student
Affairs and your academic department will assess if you have met all degree
requirements.

Note that the review of student records for degree conferral is not
automatic and only occurs if the student submits the degree
application. Students
who fail to complete the degree application by the deadline will NOT be
reviewed for degree conferral, and the degree will NOT be conferred on February
11, 2026. In such situations, the next possible degree conferral would be
in May 2026.

If you have concerns about meeting degree requirements, please reach out to
your academic advisor as soon as possible.

Sincerely,

SEAS Academics

Engineering Student Affairs

threads.subject: PAST DUE REQUIREMENT: SEAS Mandatory Orientation Tutorials

messages.subject: PAST DUE REQUIREMENT: SEAS Mandatory Orientation Tutorials
messages.body:
Dear student,

We are reaching out because you have still not completed the SEAS
Orientation Tutorials requirement in full, which is mandatory for all
Engineering graduate students. You are now past the deadline of Monday,
October 13, and will have a registration hold placed on your account next
week if you do not comply. Once the hold is placed, it will not be lifted
until the tutorials have been completed.

As a reminder, the Orientation Tutorials are available via CourseWorks and
can be accessed at the following link:
https://courseworks2.columbia.edu/courses/209703 . You must receive a
perfect score (100%) on every tutorial/quiz in order for this requirement
to be considered complete.

Please note that this requirement is in addition to the Pre-Orientation
Tutorials
<https://universitylife.columbia.edu/welcome-columbia-pre-orientation-tutorials >
and the University-wide Community Citizenship Initiative
<https://universitylife.columbia.edu/CCI >, both of which are mandated by
the Office of University Life.

Sincerely,
SEAS Academics
Engineering Student Affairs

threads.subject: SEAS Midterm Evaluations

messages.subject: SEAS Midterm Evaluations
messages.body:
Dear Karthik Sreedhar, The Engineering School values your thoughtful feedback on your courses. Please take a few minutes to fill out the Course evaluations. These evaluations are extremely important for several reasons: • They help the Engineering
ZjQcmQRYFpfptBannerStart
This Message Is From an External Sender
This message came from outside your organization.
 
ZjQcmQRYFpfptBannerEnd
 Dear Karthik Sreedhar,


The Engineering School values your thoughtful feedback on your courses. Please take a few minutes to fill out the Course evaluations. These evaluations are extremely important for several reasons:
• They help the Engineering School and Departments evaluate instructor-effectiveness
• Instructors use the feedback to improve their courses for future students
• Your feedback helps fellow students choose their courses 

An evaluation(s) for the following course(s) is ready to be filled out. This evaluation will remain open until 10/20/2025 11:59 PM. Please complete it before then.

The evaluation is available at:




Survey Summary

Course CodeCourse TitleSurvey Start DateSurvey End DateStatus
COMSE9902_048_2025_3-SEMINARINCOMPUTERSCIENCECOMSE9902_048_2025_3 - SEMINAR IN COMPUTER SCIENCE10/13/2025 12:00 AM10/20/2025 11:59 PMNot-SubmittedGo To Survey

 

Enter the site using your username and password. You may submit the evaluation once only.
If the above link is not working then please follow the Alternate Instructions at the bottom of the message.

Thank you for your time and attention in this important matter. Your feedback is very important.

Please note: Evaluations are confidential. Your instructors and TA's will never see your name associated with any evaluation responses.
------------------------------------------------------------
Should you encounter any technical difficulty in filling out the evaluation, please send an email to evaluation-seas@columbia.edu clearly indicating the problem you encountered. For any other concerns please contact your department.

Alternate Instructions:
1) Go to Login (Note: This link should not be shared with others; it is unique to you.)
2) Enter your username and password and click on 'Login' button.
3) Go to your course
4) Click on 'Evaluation' link in the left navigation menu.

threads.subject: October Carleton Commons Social this Friday! | EGSC Weekly Newsletter

messages.subject: October Carleton Commons Social this Friday! | EGSC Weekly Newsletter
messages.body:
<https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/  >
*Hey Grad SEAS!*
We hope you managed to dodge the downpour these past couple of days! With
the storm behind us and better (hopefully) weather rolling in, here's
what's upcoming with Grad SEAS!

This Friday, after our *General Body Meeting*, we're hosting our *October
Carleton Commons Social*! We're excited to see everyone there!

Don't forget to *follow us on Instagram @columbiaegsc
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.instagram.com/columbiaegsc/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusNrQV8Yo$ > *to stay updated on all our
events!
Pictures from the Fall 2025 Boat Cruise are Here!
Check Out the Album!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1xxg25tuhgV0ieB7qnsmYi9AMG8G3ySsx?usp=sharing__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0Nusp1dQVw0$ >
Join us at our *weekly GBMs*, whether you are a Department Representative
or not! We meet on Fridays from 4-5 pm in Mudd 524.
*Carleton Commons Social October Edition* is happening *this Friday*! Stop
by to grab some snacks, pizza, and drinks, and socialize with fellow SEAS
graduates!

This event is free and only for *current graduate engineering students*!
Registration is required and will take place at the door! *CUID with EP
sticker and government ID required!*
EGSC, in collaboration with SEAS graduate student organizations, is excited
to invite you to the *Fall Semester social at Ellington in the Park*!

Join us for a casual Thursday outing to enjoy the cozy fall evening!

This event includes food and drinks. Please obtain the ticket according to
your organization affiliation (i.e., if you are a Mechanical Engineering
student, you can only obtain a ticket from MEGA or EGSC). If you obtain a
ticket from the wrong organization, please notify us immediately, as your
admission to the event will not be counted!
RSVP Here!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.eventbrite.com/e/midsemester-social-tickets-1804269150599?aff=oddtdtcreator__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusR2IC0YI$ >
Interested in joining the *Coalition on Race and Ethnicity in Engineering
(CORE^2)* Eboard? Apply to be an executive member! This is a great
opportunity to utilize/gain leadership skills while simultaneously
enriching the way CORE^2 serves our members.

Join us in our mission to foster a community that provides academic,
social, and professional support for traditionally underrepresented
students in graduate engineering. We hope you are excited to fortify the
POC community in and around our university in these ways with us.
Apply Today!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSewY0za5v5I_UQXX_n_Ypt_D537mwAup-z4ZWgLm2KBdjP6SA/viewform?usp=dialog__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!TruXzRtu85xKuJPIemjZDvW0sxaZqv-xt6ZMR2O_VeI4vSVRNUjkjGOcdDHciOnzbbysr4hOKJ2Bi6rvsJ55vXcA$>
Interested in joining *Columbia’s only student club dedicated to
Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCI)*? At *Neurotech X Columbia*, you’ll find
opportunities to engage in cutting-edge research projects  and connect with
peers passionate about neurotechnology. We welcome all members of the
Columbia community to join us — please register through this Google Form:
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/u/1/d/1r6jLNyCo6pbxXR3jEqPT0a_FzcpqlB4d4iCH3_75ZbQ__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0Nusz5HwmUI$ 
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/u/1/d/1r6jLNyCo6pbxXR3jEqPT0a_FzcpqlB4d4iCH3_75ZbQ__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!U66AXcO3CK09cjy5CEinktcs0lyktt1ow3JxvVucDXBZcW_LeYTqdaFHhq0nJeCzhWoF8A1KI_e8L7oaewiVfhUko8lKWg$>
STEM Starters accepts both Columbia post-doc and graduate student
volunteers for our free science events for kids! All volunteers must go
through Protection of Minors training (~1hr) for the safety of our
attendees.

*Please record your email here
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSe6d2t9FB4bdFJtgd3aD7DeLob6h8ZbKZmVrGWJF4HjS0uQMA/viewform?pli=1__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusBO-pSA4$ >
and
we'll be in touch about next steps! *

For more information, visit our website at:
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.womeninscienceatcolumbia.org/stemstarters__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0Nus_TlCc4A$  or contact us by
email: STEMStartersCU@gmail.com.
RSVP Here!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeJDkw_2wSSNtV-ajNrS97xSEPgzuBhy2XvVXUlVSJS1dpnGg/viewform__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusV3Y0nbo$ >
Looking for a hands-on, impactful internship next summer?YC just released
its Summer 2026 internship list, featuring roles at top AI startups where
you’ll work directly with founders, tackle meaningful projects, and gain
first-hand experience building the future of AI. Many past interns have
even gone on to launch their own startups and join YC as founders! Check
out the opportunities below!
YC Summer 2026 Internship List <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://znsrc.com/c/kfmvencctd__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0Nusqfh8VbY$ >
*We are thrilled to announce that applications for Nucleate's **Flagship
Activator Program* <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.org/activator/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusWIEUJHM$ >* are **NOW OPEN!*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusSLkOpuY$ >
Are you an academic trainee (Post-doc/PhD/MBA/MD/JD/MS) eager to build a
life science startup? Whether your passion lies in *therapeutics,
diagnostics, medtech, synthetic biology*, or fields like *food, energy,
agriculture, or consumer products*, this is your opportunity to transform
your research into a groundbreaking venture!
What We Offer:
●      *Completely Free:* Nucleate never takes equity or charges fees for
its programs
●      *Mentorship from Industry Leaders:* Receive guidance from
experienced mentors.
●      *Comprehensive Educational Programming:* Learn from the best in
workshops and seminars.
●      *Global Community: *Join a network of peers, alumni, and leaders
across 18+ cities worldwide.
●      *Investor Introductions:* Get connected with investors ready to
support your breakthrough.
●      *No Forced Incorporation:* Focus on your venture at your own pace.
If you have an idea (i.e., you’re an Inventor), you can apply for either
our *Bio* or *Eco* track:
●      *Bio Track*: For innovators in therapeutics, diagnostics, or tools.
●      *Eco Track*: Focused on food & agriculture, chemicals & energy, or
consumer products.
*Don’t have an idea yet?* No worries! You can still join a team as a
*scientific,
business, or clinical contributor* through our matching process.
*Got a team already?* Your collaborators can come on board as *team
affiliates*, so you all get the chance to learn and build together.
*Applications* <https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusSLkOpuY$ >* are reviewed on a rolling
basis through October 20. Early submission is encouraged for early team
matching*.
*Check out our **FAQs page*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://drive.google.com/file/d/1WV3LQJYHbdauBCrYzPAjDUarJPJGrK5a/view__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusC42KhTw$ >* if
you have any questions*
To learn more, join us at one of the following info sessions. Please
register to attend, food and drink will be served!

*What about IP?** Nucleate Activator is an education-focused, non-dilutive
program. We do not take any equity or IP, and all programming is designed
to be non-confidential. Inventors applying to the program must secure
approval from their PI and TTO before final acceptance.*
*About Nucleate*
Nucleate <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.xyz/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusZlGqvZM$ > is a student-run nonprofit organization
that facilitates the formation of new life science ventures. Our
equity-free Activator program <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.xyz/activator/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0Nus9wOApqk$ > supports
emerging bioentrepreneurs coming from academia who plan to tackle the
biggest challenges in human health (Bio Track) and sustainability (Eco
Track) by building life science companies. Since our inception seven years
ago, we have guided 600+ scientific teams through their earliest stages,
leading to the formation of more than 180 companies that have collectively
raised over $500M. Our alumni are commercializing breakthrough technologies
such as protein sequencing (Glyphic <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://glyphic.bio/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusezQ3hpo$ >), AI drug design
(Manifold Bio <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.manifold.bio/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0Nus4qL8Sks$ >, Concerto
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.concertobio.com/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0Nus12AQYJk$ >), biomanufacturing (Foray Biosciences
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://foraybio.com/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusdrwL_DM$ >) and many more.
*Apply*
Apply here <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusMNd91Tc$ > before *October 20th, 2025*.
*To Learn more about Nucleate visit our **Linktree*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://linktr.ee/nucleatehq__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0NusQVzqgrU$ >
[image: Instagram] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://instagram.com/columbiaegsc__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0Nuse2CGxSs$ >
[image: LinkedIn] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.linkedin.com/company/egsccolumbia__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RMZHicUdx4jLe-WsXa9qiJF0KSMZrBccw9tSqcSY3-UZqbwHDqOfYxT_loGsQUBmApjgUiFL73IalemSuLlg4WhMrYbQvjP3Pqh78oDS0Nusu72KVjU$ >
[image: Website] <https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/  >
[image: Email] <communications-chair@egsc.seas.columbia.edu>
*Copyright © 2025 EGSC Columbia University, All rights reserved.*
You are receiving this email because you are a graduate student at CU SEAS.

*Our mailing address is:*
EGSC Columbia University
500 W 120th St
New York, NY 10027-6623

threads.subject: INVITATION: Symposium on AI and Finance, tomorrow, Friday, Oct. 3, 2025

messages.subject: INVITATION: Symposium on AI and Finance, tomorrow, Friday, Oct. 3, 2025
messages.body:
Dear Engineers and Scientist,

On behalf of Dean, Shih-Fu Chang, we'd like to invite you to attend
tomorrow's Symposium on AI and Finance hosted by Columbia's Center for AI
and Responsible Financial Innovation, in collaboration with Capital One.
The event will take place at Faculty House from 9am to
4:30pm, tomorrow, Friday, Oct. 3, 2025.

Learn more and register at this link
<https://cairfi.engineering.columbia.edu/events/2025-cairfi-symposium-whats-next-ai-and-finance  >.
We look forward to seeing you!

   - 9:00 | Registration & Breakfast
   - 10:00 | Introductory Remarks
      - Shih-Fu Chang, Dean, Columbia Engineering
   - 10:10 | Keynote: *Indistinguishability and the Crystal Ball*
      - Cynthia Dwork, Gordon McKay Professor of Computer Science, Harvard
      School of Engineering
   - 10:50 | Session: AI, Privacy, & Security in Finance
      - Rachel Cummings (Columbia Engineering), Junfeng Yang (Columbia
      Engineering), moderated by Stephen Rawls (Capital One)
   - 11:50 | Remarks
      - Prem Natarajan, Chief Scientist & Head of Enterprise AI, Capital One
   - 12:00 | Lunch & Poster Session
   - 1:30 | Session: AI and Causality
      - Elias Bareinboim (Columbia Engineering), Hannah Li (Columbia
      Business School), moderated by Agostino Capponi (Columbia Engineering)
   - 2:30 | Talk: The Future of Consumer Credit
      - Miao Wang (Columbia Engineering), moderated by Ali Hirsa (Columbia
      Engineering)
   - 3:00 | Panel: Future of AI & Fintech
      - Henry Yuen (Columbia Engineering), Atlas Wang (UT Austin), and
      Randall Balestriero (Brown University), moderated by Bayan Bruss (Capital
      One)
   - 4:15 | Closing Remarks

-- 

Andy Chae * He/Him/His *
Senior Director of Graduate Student Life

threads.subject: Explore Careers in Engineering & Scientific Consulting with Exponent (Oct 13)

messages.subject: Explore Careers in Engineering & Scientific Consulting with Exponent (Oct 13)
messages.body:
Dear PhD Students,

The Engineering Graduate Student Council (EGSC) is pleased to invite you to
a special seminar with Exponent, a premier engineering and scientific
consulting firm known for solving some of the world’s most complex
technical challenges.

This event is designed to help PhD students explore how their research
skills translate into impactful consulting careers. You’ll hear from
Exponent experts about the innovative projects they lead and learn how
advanced technical backgrounds contribute to solving real-world problems
across industries. It’s also a great opportunity to connect directly with
the team and learn more about career pathways and job opportunities at
Exponent.

🔹 *Date:* Monday, October 13, 2025
🔹 *Time:* 12:00 - 1:00 PM
🔹 *Location:* Hamilton 603
🔹 Lunch will be provided

*Featured Speakers:*

   -

   *Dr. Courtney Petersen* - *Senior Associate, Biomedical Engineering &
   Sciences*
   Focused on medical device evaluation and MRI compatibility through
   computational and experimental methods.
   -

   *Dr. Khaled Hashad* - *Senior Engineer, Thermal Sciences*
   Specializes in CFD, heat transfer, and machine learning applications for
   failure analysis and thermal management.
   -

   *Ms. Sarah Dahlberg* - *Managing Scientist, Health Sciences*
   Expert in regulatory affairs, pharmacovigilance, and human health risk
   assessment for pharmaceuticals and biotechnology.

Whether you’re curious about consulting or actively exploring your next
step after graduate school, this seminar offers valuable insight into how
Exponent’s collaborative environment empowers PhD students to thrive in the
industry.

👉 *RSVP here:* Register Now
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://exponentrecruit.azurewebsites.net/Register/302__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!SV5xJ-dRS6Hnt9qaseBWp5ys9ejsyo0RG-WmV4-AyB8FZo9pmXJg7T_lFxmtINlQVG8P-fFr7NIoIh9YaGjtG5Ok3QnyLvP5lwqhDFiFSvK-mK1nhcgiO7s$ >
[image: Columbia - Exponent Seminar - October 13. 2025 - Flyer
(Updated).jpg]

*Kindly see the attached flyer for additional details.*

We hope to see you there!

*With Regards, Dhruv Bhatt, M.S.*
* <http://egsc.seas.columbia.edu/  >*
*Career & Alumni Chair, *
*Engineering Graduate Student Co <http://egsc.seas.columbia.edu/  >**uncil
(EGSC <http://egsc.seas.columbia.edu/  >)*
*Columbia Engineering, Mechanical Engineering*
*He/ Him/ His*

threads.subject: October 30 Faculty Panel - Invitation

messages.subject: October 30 Faculty Panel - Invitation
messages.body:
Dear Doctoral Students,

We are pleased to share the upcoming session, *Faculty Lives, Faculty
Careers: Stories from the Academic Job Market*, part of Columbia
University’s *Academic Application & Early Career Success Series*,
organized by the Office for Postdoctoral Affairs and the Zuckerman
Institute.
*Thursday, October 30, 2025*
*3:30 – 5:00 PM*
*Jerome L. Greene Science Center, 9th Floor*
* RSVP HERE
<https://events.columbia.edu/cal/event/eventView.do?b=de&calPath=/public/cals/MainCal&guid=CAL-00bbdb71-9970783f-0199-72646c8a-00004d15events@columbia.edu&recurrenceId=  >*
(Registration closes October 29 at 12:00 PM)

This 90-minute panel will bring together faculty at different career stages
to share their firsthand experiences navigating the academic job market,
from preparing applications to interviewing and transitioning into new
roles. Attendees will gain practical insights and strategies for success at
each stage of the process.

Please note: This session will not be recorded.

Best,

*Richard Hagen, Ph.D.* *(He/Him)*
Assistant Director, Graduate Student Affairs
Engineering Student Affairs
Columbia Engineering
