threads.subject: LinkedIn Photoshoot, Cocoa House, and PDS | EGSC Weekly Newsletter

messages.subject: LinkedIn Photoshoot, Cocoa House, and PDS | EGSC Weekly Newsletter
messages.body:
<https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/  >
*Hey Grad SEAS!*

It was great seeing everyone at the *Fall 2025* *Boat Cruise* last Friday!
We all had such a great time 🫰🩵🦁💙🫰

*📸 *Tickets for today's *LinkedIn Photoshoot* are *SOLD OUT 📸*
🍪☕️ We are hosting *EGSC Cocoa House* on *Thursday *☕️🍪
📖 Apply for the *Professional Development Scholarship* by *October 11th* 📖

Don't forget to *follow us on Instagram @columbiaegsc
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.instagram.com/columbiaegsc/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHfSPMDko$ > *to stay updated on all our
events!
*Pictures from the Graduate Campus Life Fair are Here!*
View the Album Here!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/11FCvPNSHrN6gHUwS58HSvVtNKpb-b92u?usp=sharing__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHCb-yPRc$ >
Tickets for the *LinkedIn Photoshoot* are *SOLD OUT*! Please stay tuned for
more opportunities to get your professional headshot taken throughout the
semester!
*Caffeinate and conversate* with other students from graduate SEAS! Enjoy
coffee, cookies, and some great company *this Thursday* at *EGSC Cocoa
House*!
Join us at our *weekly GBMs*, whether you are a Department Representative
or not! We meet on Fridays from 4-5 pm in Mudd 524.

Are you a current Columbia University graduate student founder eager to
pitch your business idea? Or are you looking to connect with other founders
and like-minded peers? If so, join us for an inspiring evening of pitches,
networking, and conversation!

Co-sponsored by the *Lang Center at Columbia Business School *and* the
Engineering Graduate Student Council*, this event offers a unique
opportunity to share ideas, meet fellow entrepreneurs, and build meaningful
connections across Columbia’s schools.

How It Works:

*When you register, you’ll answer a few quick questions—including whether
you’d like to pitch. *If selected, you’ll have the chance to showcase your
idea and meet potential collaborators. After the pitches, we’ll transition
into a relaxed mixer where you can continue networking.

This is a *casual and welcoming* environment—just bring yourself and your
enthusiasm! Whether you're actively working on a venture or simply curious,
we’d love to have you join us.

We look forward to seeing you there!

*THE ENTREPRENEURSHIP MIXER & PITCH NIGHT IS SOLD OUT!*
Join the Waitlist Here! <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/jsoog0en__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHR4F_m4k$ >
In keeping with the highest ideals of the EGSC, the *Professional
Development Scholarship Program* is awarded to outstanding graduate
students in the Columbia University Fu Foundation School of Engineering and
Applied Science (SEAS) as a means of defraying costs associated with their
professional endeavors.  Those chosen as EGSC Professional Development
Scholars are expected to make a broader impact on the larger Columbia
Engineering community by sharing their knowledge and experience.

Past recipients have used the Scholarship to fund *memberships* *to
professional societies, certification exam fees, and the costs of attending
academic or professional conferences*.

*AWARD AMOUNT:*
Applicants may request *up to $1,000* per application. The final award
amount and the number of awards per semester will be decided by the
evaluating committee. Any unused funds are non-transferable and may not be
used for expenses unrelated to those explicitly stated in the funded
application.

Please note that original receipts and proof of acceptance/attendance (if
applicable) are required in order to be eligible for reimbursement.
Furthermore, for travel expenses, stays exceeding 1 day before and after
the event must be justified and will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

*FALL APPLICATION DEADLINE:*
*Saturday, October 11th, 2025 at 5:00PM EST.*
Apply Here!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScukPIxcTNSLHAOkoMMPw8z1UPCiQgjb6sLuwFhgsV7JEPqDQ/viewform__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHn65Mc2w$ >
Columbia Product Managers Club (PMC) and Columbia Entrepreneurship,
Leadership & Consulting (ELC) Club invite you to an evening of networking
and discovery.

Whether you’re passionate about *product management, consulting, or
entrepreneurship*, this mixer is the perfect place to connect with peers,
exchange ideas, and explore future opportunities.

✨ Expect meaningful conversations, new connections across disciplines, and
a chance to learn from fellow students shaping careers at the intersection
of tech, business, and innovation.

🥂 *Food & Drinks Provided* – come for the networking, stay for the good
vibes!

📍 *Location: *The Craftsman
📅 *Date:* Wednesday, October 1st, 2025
🕡 *Time:* 6:30 – 7:30 PM

🤝 *Hosted by:* Columbia Product Managers Club (PMC) x Columbia
Entrepreneurship, Leadership & Consulting (ELC) Club.

🔗 RSVP now — *scan the QR code on the poster!*

📨 Stay tuned and follow us for updates:
📧 *pmccolumbia@gmail.com <pmccolumbia@gmail.com>*
📸 Instagram: @columbiapmc
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://instagram.com/columbiapmc__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Wa1FqtnIYlf9dXmH8b4FCUl4MnoVUxEHEIhbSo2YJ3_M4dwQAytWcrQfPCFORNNSg-tSEqPRrB8KrO_IebEVvuJ01UlIZ7df0im08YQ$>
💼 LinkedIn: Columbia Product Managers Club (PMC)
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.linkedin.com/company/columbia-pmc__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Wa1FqtnIYlf9dXmH8b4FCUl4MnoVUxEHEIhbSo2YJ3_M4dwQAytWcrQfPCFORNNSg-tSEqPRrB8KrO_IebEVvuJ01UlIZ7dfBe5eENk$>
Interested in joining the *Coalition on Race and Ethnicity in Engineering
(CORE^2)* Eboard? Apply to be an executive member! This is a great
opportunity to utilize/gain leadership skills while simultaneously
enriching the way CORE^2 serves our members.

Join us in our mission to foster a community that provides academic,
social, and professional support for traditionally underrepresented
students in graduate engineering. We hope you are excited to fortify the
POC community in and around our university in these ways with us.
Apply Today!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSewY0za5v5I_UQXX_n_Ypt_D537mwAup-z4ZWgLm2KBdjP6SA/viewform?usp=dialog__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!TruXzRtu85xKuJPIemjZDvW0sxaZqv-xt6ZMR2O_VeI4vSVRNUjkjGOcdDHciOnzbbysr4hOKJ2Bi6rvsJ55vXcA$>
STEM Starters accepts both Columbia post-doc and graduate student
volunteers for our free science events for kids! All volunteers must go
through Protection of Minors training (~1hr) for the safety of our
attendees.

*Please record your email here
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSe6d2t9FB4bdFJtgd3aD7DeLob6h8ZbKZmVrGWJF4HjS0uQMA/viewform?pli=1__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHPCVHjUU$ >
and
we'll be in touch about next steps! *

For more information, visit our website at:
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.womeninscienceatcolumbia.org/stemstarters__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzH9zuYmtM$  or contact us by
email: STEMStartersCU@gmail.com.

*** No-cost flu vaccines at these events are available to ALL ELIGIBLE
STUDENTS* <https://www.health.columbia.edu/flu#text-18266  > regardless of
credit load or health insurance plan.No appointment needed: just bring your
CUID.***

*Monday, Sep. 29 - Wednesday, Oct. 1: Low Library Rotunda*
*Enter via the Northeast entrance only.
<https://www.health.columbia.edu/sites/default/files/content/pics/Flu-Fair/Map%20to%20NE%20Entrance%20from%20Sundial.png  >*
Monday, Sep. 29: 10:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m.
Tuesday, Sep. 30: 7:30 a.m. - 4:30 p.m.
Wednesday, Oct. 1: 9:00 a.m - 6:30 p.m.

*Wednesday, October 8: 3rd Fl. Board Rooms, Geffen Hall (Manhattanville)*
9:00 a.m. - 3:00 p.m.

Please note that you are not able to join the line prior to opening or
after closing.

For those over 65 years of age
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.cdc.gov/flu/highrisk/65over.htm__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X7f859X_d379DEJVUzq_6MK7S-OiquHc7Pmxyfto6wyujDDIv8qCILkURv8l3bSzvpIpCPWNTPJ17JFIMV1EVglHHG9ALunTdXs$>,
Columbia Health has a limited supply of the high-dose vaccine. Please speak
to the nurse administering your vaccine if you are eligible for the
high-dose flu vaccine.

***Early bird exclusive****
Each day, the first 150 community members who get their flu shot at the
event will receive an exclusive “Fight Flu” hot/cold pack!
*CTV, Columbia’s technology transfer office, is hiring graduate students
for the Fellows Program
<https://techventures.columbia.edu/about-ctv/ctv-fellows-program  >*. This is
a unique internship opportunity that introduces scientists to technology
commercialization. Fellows work closely with CTV on a wide range of
projects, including assessment of the commercial viability of new
inventions, identifying potential licensees or investors, developing
marketing materials, and analysis of patent literature. Fellows are paid
$28.46 per hour.

*Applications for the Fellows Program opened on 9/17/2025*. The first round
of applications is due *10/2/2025 by 5 pm EDT*. Info sessions will be held
on Sept. 15 at 3 pm and Sept. 18 at 10 am. Both will be Zoom meetings held
on this link: https://columbiauniversity.zoom.us/my/ctvfellows  .

Looking for a hands-on, impactful internship next summer?YC just released
its Summer 2026 internship list, featuring roles at top AI startups where
you’ll work directly with founders, tackle meaningful projects, and gain
first-hand experience building the future of AI. Many past interns have
even gone on to launch their own startups and join YC as founders! Check
out the opportunities below!
YC Summer 2026 Internship List <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://znsrc.com/c/kfmvencctd__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHzsXekPs$ >
*We are thrilled to announce that applications for Nucleate's **Flagship
Activator Program* <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.org/activator/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHCA2WLv8$ >* are **NOW OPEN!*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHecHcL24$ >
Are you an academic trainee (Post-doc/PhD/MBA/MD/JD/MS) eager to build a
life science startup? Whether your passion lies in *therapeutics,
diagnostics, medtech, synthetic biology*, or fields like *food, energy,
agriculture, or consumer products*, this is your opportunity to transform
your research into a groundbreaking venture!
What We Offer:
●      *Completely Free:* Nucleate never takes equity or charges fees for
its programs
●      *Mentorship from Industry Leaders:* Receive guidance from
experienced mentors.
●      *Comprehensive Educational Programming:* Learn from the best in
workshops and seminars.
●      *Global Community: *Join a network of peers, alumni, and leaders
across 18+ cities worldwide.
●      *Investor Introductions:* Get connected with investors ready to
support your breakthrough.
●      *No Forced Incorporation:* Focus on your venture at your own pace.
If you have an idea (i.e., you’re an Inventor), you can apply for either
our *Bio* or *Eco* track:
●      *Bio Track*: For innovators in therapeutics, diagnostics, or tools.
●      *Eco Track*: Focused on food & agriculture, chemicals & energy, or
consumer products.
*Don’t have an idea yet?* No worries! You can still join a team as a
*scientific,
business, or clinical contributor* through our matching process.
*Got a team already?* Your collaborators can come on board as *team
affiliates*, so you all get the chance to learn and build together.
*Applications* <https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHecHcL24$ >* are reviewed on a rolling
basis through October 20. Early submission is encouraged for early team
matching*.
*Check out our **FAQs page*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://drive.google.com/file/d/1WV3LQJYHbdauBCrYzPAjDUarJPJGrK5a/view__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHXUyRaxk$ >* if
you have any questions*
To learn more, join us at one of the following info sessions. Please
register to attend, food and drink will be served!

   1. Columbia Morningside
   <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/64sdaqgf?utm_source=columbia-group__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHK6vT-YY$ >Info Session:
   <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/64sdaqgf?utm_source=columbia-group__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHK6vT-YY$ > Tuesday, September
   30, 5:30 PM at Davis Auditorium
   2. Columbia Medical Center
   <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/xwnoz3hs?utm_source=columbia-group__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHDcSlz8U$ > Info Session:
   <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/xwnoz3hs?utm_source=columbia-group__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHDcSlz8U$ > Thursday, October
   2, 5:30 PM at Alexandria LaunchLabs

Can’t make the in-person session? *Sign up for a virtual info session or
Live Q&A session with Activator Alumni and Mentors*:

   1. Virtual Info Session:
   <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/n5t938v2?utm_source=columbia-group__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHcCE5rME$ >  Thursday,
   September 18, at 12 PM
   2. Nucleate Activator - Insider Session:
   <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/pcjqooak?utm_source=columbia-group__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzH6W0dgWY$ > Tuesday, October
   7, at 6 PM

Alternatively, join us at one of our *events*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://lu.ma/nucleate-ny-community-events__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzH2oNC3o4$ > in NYC!

   1. *Connect and Collab: Fall Mixer with NYAS*
   <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/c4fiyh8y?utm_source=columbia-group__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHkOypDBU$ > Thursday,
   September 25th, 6 PM at NYAS

*What about IP?** Nucleate Activator is an education-focused, non-dilutive
program. We do not take any equity or IP, and all programming is designed
to be non-confidential. Inventors applying to the program must secure
approval from their PI and TTO before final acceptance.*
*About Nucleate*
Nucleate <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.xyz/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHqgN4iV8$ > is a student-run nonprofit organization
that facilitates the formation of new life science ventures. Our
equity-free Activator program <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.xyz/activator/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHNUQunGI$ > supports
emerging bioentrepreneurs coming from academia who plan to tackle the
biggest challenges in human health (Bio Track) and sustainability (Eco
Track) by building life science companies. Since our inception seven years
ago, we have guided 600+ scientific teams through their earliest stages,
leading to the formation of more than 180 companies that have collectively
raised over $500M. Our alumni are commercializing breakthrough technologies
such as protein sequencing (Glyphic <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://glyphic.bio/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHAKoyiLE$ >), AI drug design
(Manifold Bio <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.manifold.bio/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHsNDABnQ$ >, Concerto
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.concertobio.com/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHWWFzlvU$ >), biomanufacturing (Foray Biosciences
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://foraybio.com/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHR_jElTk$ >) and many more.
*Apply*
Apply here <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzH9n9veE0$ > before *October 20th, 2025*.
*To Learn more about Nucleate visit our **Linktree*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://linktr.ee/nucleatehq__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHjhitSBI$ >
[image: Instagram] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://instagram.com/columbiaegsc__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHx5uMQX8$ >
[image: LinkedIn] <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.linkedin.com/company/egsccolumbia__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RprEPbdeups-V8v9_epVBFA0EDqZTNKig576zdJN80QDbpEW8CclHc-1M8Dv4hKc0K8u9A3N9cNAwSnUBb23SJqvh1W2uwMkywkOeV7ozPzHn48dQv0$ >

threads.subject: Get your free flu shot next week at Low Library!

messages.subject: Get your free flu shot next week at Low Library!
messages.body:
Dear Karthik,

  

Following up on the message sent by Dr. Bernitz, I’d like to encourage you to get your no-cost flu shot next week. The flu virus is already circulating in New York City, and the best way to protect ourselves and our family, friends, and colleagues is to get the flu shot.

  

Starting next Monday Sep. 29 through Wednesday Oct. 1, you can go to Low Library to get a no-cost flu shot. Walk-in only, just bring your CUID. If you can’t make it next week, there is an additional opportunity on the Manhattanville campus on Oct. 8.

  

Monday, Sep. 29 - Wednesday, Oct. 1: Low Library Rotunda

Enter via the Northeast entrance only.

Monday, Sep. 29: 10:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m.

Tuesday, Sep. 30: 7:30 a.m. - 4:30 p.m.

Wednesday, Oct. 1: 9:00 a.m - 6:30 p.m.

  

Wednesday, Oct. 8: 3rd Fl. Board Rooms, Geffen Hall

9:00 a.m. - 3:00 p.m.

   

Please note that you are not able to join the line prior to opening or after closing.

  

If you have specific concerns about receiving a flu vaccine, please connect with us at Medical Services: call 212-854-7426, ext. 4 and request to speak with a care team member who will provide guidance.


Thank you for taking this opportunity to keep yourself and our community healthy.

  

Be well,

Urmi Desai
Associate Vice President, Medical Services

messages.body:
This event has been updated
Changed: title, description

🌮 DAPLab Seminar and Lunch - Guest Speaker Kyunghyun Cho
Tuesday Sep 30, 2025 ⋅ 12pm – 1:30pm
Eastern Time - New York

Location
453 Mudd/CSB	
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.google.com/maps/search/453*Mudd*2FCSB?hl=en__;KyU!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RwosTY2AM6qNj9ts4JVbhhArQJdfTRv4EujH36PkKLOdOmQZDsu42lKnrXI40r2-LuvDtxNZSfp2uOnLzZaxGkvZ$ 

Attachments
2025 Fall DAPLab  Seminar Agenda  
https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1wUzL8H6R6DUbXh2hhsI64l7lvNFddWCaFViEZ_Ryn10/edit?slide=id.g37d27ac9933_0_1__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RwosTY2AM6qNj9ts4JVbhhArQJdfTRv4EujH36PkKLOdOmQZDsu42lKnrXI40r2-LuvDtxNZSfp2uOnLzTSSV8QQ$ 

2025 Fall DAPLab  Seminar AgendaAbstractDespite its amazing success,  
leaderboard chasing has become something researchers dread and mock. When  
implemented properly and executed faithfully, leaderboard chasing can lead  
to both faster and easily reproducible progress in science, as evident from  
the amazing progress we have seen with machine learning, or more broadly  
artificial intelligence, in recent decades. It does not however mean that  
it is easy to implement and execute leaderboard chasing properly. In this  
talk, I will go over four case studies demonstrating the issues that  
ultimately prevent leaderboard chasing from a valid scientific approach.  
The first case study is on the lack of proper hyperparameter tuning in  
continual learning, the second on the lack of consensus on evaluation  
metrics in machine unlearning, the third on the challenges of properly  
evaluating the evaluation metrics in free-form text generation, and the  
final one on wishful thinking. By going over these cases, I hope we can  
collectively acknowledge some of our own fallacies, think of underlying  
causes behind these fallacies and come up with better ways to approach  
artificial intelligence research.Speaker BioKyunghyun Cho is a professor of  
computer science and data science at New York University and an executive  
director of frontier research at the Prescient Design team within Genentech  
Research & Early Development (gRED). He became the Glen de Vries  
Professor of Health Statistics in 2025. He is also a CIFAR Fellow of  
Learning in Machines & Brains and an Associate Member of the National  
Academy of Engineering of Korea. He served as a (co-)Program Chair of ICLR  
2020, NeurIPS 2022 and ICML 2022. He was one of the three founding  
Editors-in-Chief of the Transactions on Machine Learning Research (TMLR)  
until 2024. He was a research scientist at Facebook AI Research from June  
2017 to May 2020 and a postdoctoral fellow at University of Montreal until  
Summer 2015 under the supervision of Prof. Yoshua Bengio, after receiving  
MSc and PhD degrees from Aalto University April 2011 and April 2014,  
respectively, under the supervision of Prof. Juha Karhunen, Dr. Tapani  
Raiko and Dr. Alexander Ilin. He received the Samsung Ho-Am Prize in  
Engineering in 2021. He tries his best to find a balance among machine  
learning, natural language processing, and life, but almost always fails to  
do so.

threads.subject: EGSC Boat Cruise – Important Security & Documentation Reminder

messages.subject: EGSC Boat Cruise – Important Security & Documentation Reminder
messages.body:
Dear Students,

We hope you are excited for tonight’s EGSC Boat Cruise! To help ensure
everything runs smoothly, please keep the following security reminders in
mind:

   -

   *Do not bring items that could be viewed as weapons* (e.g., mace,
   scissors, or other sharp objects).
   -

   *Avoid overly large bags or backpacks* to help ensure a quick and smooth
   security check.
   -

   *Bring only physical, government-issued identification* (passport, visa,
   or driver’s license). *Digital copies of age verification documents will
   not be accepted.*

We can’t wait to see you tonight!

Sincerely,

Seas-Life

messages.subject: Study Sign-Up Confirmation : September 29, 2025 12:00 PM
messages.body:
This email is to notify you that you have been signed up for the following study by Lucie Clifford:

Study Name: Velmara Study
Date: Monday, September 29, 2025 12:00 PM - 12:07 PM
Location: Behavioral Research Lab (Multipurpose, Uris B204)
Researchers: Derek Brown <ndb2137@gsb.columbia.edu>, Caden Burkman <crb2227@columbia.edu>, Sophie Cheng <sqc2103@barnard.edu>, Sania Cunningham <sc5399@barnard.edu>, Zahra Kamal <zhk2108@barnard.edu>, Aria Simon <mbs2252@barnard.edu>

You may cancel your sign-up until Sunday, September 28, 2025 12:00 PM.

If you have any further questions, you may contact the researcher or logon to the site for more information: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia-brl.sona-systems.com__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!WKnFy2lcrbwYwsT_82POc7NRMFYw3bYYaiIMTE7bD0HnB7szlGqZybj7ZWeucUDTi5Jhj7Rg8gMlJ9Q6leotGqdyHCUEEIjcpYVd0Q$ 

NOTE: You are only identified to researchers by your Identity Code so be sure to provide them with this code when you attend the study: 155638

Thank you for signing up to participate in research in the Behavioral Research Lab. Please arrive to the study on time. You will not be allowed to participate if you are more than 5 minutes late. 

In the event of a last minute emergency that prevents you from attending your time slot, and you're unable to cancel your time slot on SONA, please email the researchers on the study and inform them that you will not be able to attend. 

Directions can be found here:
http://www8.gsb.columbia.edu/behaviorlab/directions 

Video directions: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://youtu.be/mcjJ_zddhTc__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!WKnFy2lcrbwYwsT_82POc7NRMFYw3bYYaiIMTE7bD0HnB7szlGqZybj7ZWeucUDTi5Jhj7Rg8gMlJ9Q6leotGqdyHCUEEIi0KuB_Pw$ 

Please take note of the following lab locations:

Rooms B203, B204, and B205: Located in the BASEMENT of Uris Hall, through an exterior door outside of the Deli (under the stairs). They cannot be accessed from inside Uris and are NOT LOCATED on the second floor of Uris Hall. 

Eligibility Requirements: CU ID Holders Only
Brief Description: Participate in a quick 7-min study. After completing a simple task, fill out a short Qualtrics survey and you will receive a $5 Amazon gift card.

threads.subject: Flybridge Columbia Breakfast: Connect with Startups & Talent

messages.subject: Flybridge Columbia Breakfast: Connect with Startups & Talent
messages.body:
Hi All,

This is Daniel ( https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.linkedin.com/in/danielporrasr/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!WbjjzOvnVjtMF5QsxIWOXN27XmX_w0q20nfLMg7NcUpsKEsO4NpH_mcHR5xHSAtXnP6ZSojDLpaEmd0LAjds8hk$  ) from Flybridge. You previously attended or registered to our  Columbia research breakfast. I recently shared highlights ( https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.linkedin.com/posts/danielporrasr_inside-the-room-with-columbia-university-activity-7348712645924999168-L7eM?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAABXZT0IBQfwUsHtDrJxgY4zEVxG-9CmX1_Y__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!WbjjzOvnVjtMF5QsxIWOXN27XmX_w0q20nfLMg7NcUpsKEsO4NpH_mcHR5xHSAtXnP6ZSojDLpaEmd0LuSvSMd0$  ) from our discussion (on RL environments and data retrieval challenges).

We’ve been hosting these events with researchers/engineers from Columbia, MIT, and NYU, and I’ve noticed strong interest from people exploring research and engineering roles at AI startups, as well as from founders looking to hire technical talent. To help facilitate connections, I created a short form ( https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://tech-hires.replit.app/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!WbjjzOvnVjtMF5QsxIWOXN27XmX_w0q20nfLMg7NcUpsKEsO4NpH_mcHR5xHSAtXnP6ZSojDLpaEmd0LanldlMo$  ) to match talent with opportunities across our 100+ portfolio companies and broader founder network.

If you’re interested in exploring opportunities at AI startups (pre-seed to Series B) or if you’re a founder looking to hire, you can fill out the form here ( https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://tech-hires.replit.app/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!WbjjzOvnVjtMF5QsxIWOXN27XmX_w0q20nfLMg7NcUpsKEsO4NpH_mcHR5xHSAtXnP6ZSojDLpaEmd0LanldlMo$  ). All information *will remain confidential* and only shared when there’s a potential match. Mostly starting this to match amazing talent with amazing companies.

Best,

Daniel

threads.subject: REMINDER: SEAS Mandatory Orientation Tutorials

messages.subject: REMINDER: SEAS Mandatory Orientation Tutorials
messages.body:
Dear student,

We are reaching out with a second reminder in regard to the SEAS
Orientation Tutorials requirement, which is mandatory for all SEAS graduate
students.

At this time, our records indicate that you have either not begun the
tutorials or have not yet successfully completed all of them. We are kindly
asking that you complete this SEAS requirement by Monday, October 13th,
2025. If you do not complete the Orientation Tutorials by this date, you
will receive a registration hold that will prevent you from registering for
future courses. Once placed, the hold will not be lifted until the
tutorials have been completed.

The Orientation Tutorials are available on Courseworks via the following
course link: https://courseworks2.columbia.edu/courses/209703 . Please note
that this requirement is in addition to the pre-orientation tutorials
<https://universitylife.columbia.edu/welcome-columbia-pre-orientation-tutorials >
and the university-wide Community Citizenship Initiative
<https://universitylife.columbia.edu/CCI > (CCI) mandated by the Office of
University Life.

As previously noted, you must receive a perfect score (100%) on each
tutorial/quiz in order for this requirement to be considered complete. You
may re-take quizzes as needed until you achieve a perfect score. Kindly
note that the Food Pantry quiz is optional but strongly recommended.

Let us know of any questions or concerns that you might have.

Kind regards,

SEAS Academics

Engineering Student Affairs

FINDINGS: Procedure was done after informed consent, with aseptic technique and under fluoroscopic guidance. 20-gauge spinal needle was inserted beneath the left L5 pedicle. 2 mL of Omnipaque 300 were injected with digital subtraction fluoroscopy with perineural and epidural spread of contrast. 80 mg of Kenalog and 2 mL of lidocaine 1% were injected. No complications during the procedure. Patient tolerated the procedure well.

Results - FL GUIDED EPIDURAL LUMBAR SACRAL 1 LEVEL LEFT - Final result (09/20/2024 12:26 PM EDT)
Procedure Note
Robert Schneider - 09/20/2024 
Formatting of this note might be different from the original.

HISTORY: Low back pain extending down the left leg. MRI 04/17/2024 showed L4-5 disc protrusion with impingement of the subarticular recesses, left worse than right

FINDINGS: Procedure was done after informed consent, with aseptic technique and under fluoroscopic guidance. 20-gauge spinal needle was inserted beneath the left L5 pedicle. 2 mL of Omnipaque 300 were injected with digital subtraction fluoroscopy with perineural and epidural spread of contrast. 80 mg of Kenalog and 2 mL of lidocaine 1% were injected. No complications during the procedure. Patient tolerated the procedure well.

IMPRESSION:
Left L5-S1 transforaminal epidural injection of corticosteroid and local anesthetic.

threads.subject: RSVP – Crafting Your Chalk Talk (October 30, 2025)

messages.subject: RSVP – Crafting Your Chalk Talk (October 30, 2025)
messages.body:
Dear Doctoral Students,

We are excited to share an opportunity organized by the Office for
Postdoctoral Affairs and the Zuckerman Institute. Please see below for
details:

   -

   Event: Crafting Your Chalk Talk
   -

   Date: October 30, 2025
   -

   Time: 1:00–2:00 PM
   -

   Speaker: Professor Allie Obermeyer, PhD
   -

   Building: Schapiro CEPSR
   -

   Room: 750
   -

   RSVP Here
   <https://events.columbia.edu/cal/event/eventView.do?b=de&calPath=/public/cals/MainCal&guid=CAL-00bbdb71-9970783f-0199-7279cbc3-00005177events@columbia.edu&recurrenceId=  >

Professor Obermeyer will discuss what to expect during chalk talks for
faculty positions, including what search committees look for in both
candidates and presentations. She will also provide guidance and strategies
for developing and delivering a compelling chalk talk that effectively
communicates your expertise, vision, and fit for the role.

For more information, please contact postdocaffairs@columbia.edu.

Best,
*Richard Hagen, Ph.D.* *(He/Him)*
Assistant Director, Graduate Student Affairs
Engineering Student Affairs
Columbia Engineering

I found this research paper very interesting: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.03348__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!Ryywz3qV8VRdn3zKFZGZGCkDn8kt1RlSxh9GWBaaK7pXw4J7lrX84StvA5rcObBEjZJSqEKN8eLcae0YQnA9yw$  The passage explains the technological evolution of automated bidding systems in real-time bidding (RTB) advertising, with a particular focus on the new paradigm of Generative Reinforcement Learning (GRL) introduced by Kuaishou. Now, the field has entered the fourth generation of Generative Reinforcement Learning. This approach integrates generative models (such as Transformers or Diffusion models), which are good at understanding and producing complex sequences, with reinforcement learning, which directly optimizes long-term value. Together, they allow the system to make multi-dimensional, sequence-aware decisions.

threads.subject: BOAT CRUISE THIS FRIDAY! | EGSC Weekly Newsletter

messages.subject: BOAT CRUISE THIS FRIDAY! | EGSC Weekly Newsletter
messages.body:
<https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/  >
*Hey Grad SEAS!*

The wait is almost over! The *Fall 2025 Boat Cruise sets sail this Friday!* 🌊
We hope you got your tickets and are ready for a night of *music*, *food*,
*friends*, and *incredible NYC views*. See you on board!

There is *no General Body Meeting this week!* See you at the *Boat Cruise!*

Don't forget to *follow us on Instagram @columbiaegsc
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.instagram.com/columbiaegsc/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmqo_z2FW4$ > *to stay updated on all our
events!
*This Friday* is set for an unforgettable night on the water! Join us for
an evening of stunning skyline views, great music, and endless memories as
we set sail on the *Fall 2025 EGSC Boat Cruise*!

Want to hear your favorite songs while dancing on a boat? Request them here
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__forms.gle_bvTe77Gw4rThx4bk7&d=DwMFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=Cb_CSEMAkvP3tyUqTDqTw4Y24I3JZggiCZhfegGkp9g&m=qAGIZSj-vvk5gTYUbBR2E5MvK5jQrEsY_sxJkmBA8ntbSfHUUnxPVAWVhwy84lRC&s=TsWqJctkV3qC2SyzMLYm7zubobQEsuadeX3r9Xrt0bg&e=  >
so
our DJ can add them to the mix! 🕺💃
SEAS graduate students are invited to take part in another free *LinkedIn
Headshot Photoshoot* on September 30th from 11:00 pm – 5:00 pm. This
semester, the photoshoot is planned to take place outdoors for a fresh,
professional look.

All participants are required to check in at CEPSR 414 at Columbia
University before their session. Further details about the exact photoshoot
location will be shared at check-in. Please note that the event format
(outdoor vs. indoor) is subject to change depending on weather conditions.
If we are forced to move indoors due to weather, no one is to be held
responsible.

*Important Details:*
-Spots are limited and first-come-first-serve.
-You must select a specific time slot (no slot exchanges are possible).
-Each student may register for only one slot and must do so for themselves.

*This event is open exclusively to SEAS graduate students.*
- You must register with your Columbia email; other registrations will be
canceled.
- Please arrive 5 minutes prior to your time slot. Each session will take
about 9-10 minutes, but schedules may run behind.
- Dress professionally as you would like to appear in your headshot.
- Photos will be shared within 3–4 weeks of the event; any delays will be
communicated by email.

*At check-in, bring:*
- Your Eventbrite ticket
- Your Columbia ID with EP sticker
- Multiple reservations and registrations by non-SEAS graduate students
will be canceled.
Register Here!
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.eventbrite.com/e/1721578179679?aff=oddtdtcreator__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!WJOgu7uKDr7e5ZIEmmaqHsrsRa2KtvbWE9ZhlqKoaghGKjqcyWBWQmxLXrcnFf4w_nFjYJIhgeUCbuSMdJP1RAVziGgTiCiROhvDx6sc$>
*Caffeinate and conversate* with other students from graduate SEAS! Enjoy
coffee, cookies, and some great company *next Thursday* at *EGSC Cocoa
House*!

Are you a current Columbia University graduate student founder eager to
pitch your business idea? Or are you looking to connect with other founders
and like-minded peers? If so, join us for an inspiring evening of pitches,
networking, and conversation!

Co-sponsored by the *Lang Center at Columbia Business School *and* the
Engineering Graduate Student Council*, this event offers a unique
opportunity to share ideas, meet fellow entrepreneurs, and build meaningful
connections across Columbia’s schools.

How It Works:

*When you register, you’ll answer a few quick questions—including whether
you’d like to pitch. *If selected, you’ll have the chance to showcase your
idea and meet potential collaborators. After the pitches, we’ll transition
into a relaxed mixer where you can continue networking.

This is a *casual and welcoming* environment—just bring yourself and your
enthusiasm! Whether you're actively working on a venture or simply curious,
we’d love to have you join us.

We look forward to seeing you there!
Register Here! <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/jsoog0en__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmqVuXtHhg$ >

*** No-cost flu vaccines at these events are available to ALL ELIGIBLE
STUDENTS* <https://www.health.columbia.edu/flu#text-18266  > regardless of
credit load or health insurance plan.No appointment needed: just bring your
CUID.***

*Monday, Sep. 29 - Wednesday, Oct. 1: Low Library Rotunda*
*Enter via the Northeast entrance only.
<https://www.health.columbia.edu/sites/default/files/content/pics/Flu-Fair/Map%20to%20NE%20Entrance%20from%20Sundial.png  >*
Monday, Sep. 29: 10:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m.
Tuesday, Sep. 30: 7:30 a.m. - 4:30 p.m.
Wednesday, Oct. 1: 9:00 a.m - 6:30 p.m.

*Wednesday, October 8: 3rd Fl. Board Rooms, Geffen Hall (Manhattanville)*
9:00 a.m. - 3:00 p.m.

Please note that you are not able to join the line prior to opening or
after closing.

For those over 65 years of age
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.cdc.gov/flu/highrisk/65over.htm__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!X7f859X_d379DEJVUzq_6MK7S-OiquHc7Pmxyfto6wyujDDIv8qCILkURv8l3bSzvpIpCPWNTPJ17JFIMV1EVglHHG9ALunTdXs$>,
Columbia Health has a limited supply of the high-dose vaccine. Please speak
to the nurse administering your vaccine if you are eligible for the
high-dose flu vaccine.

***Early bird exclusive****
Each day, the first 150 community members who get their flu shot at the
event will receive an exclusive “Fight Flu” hot/cold pack!
🚀 *Entrepreneur + Product Manager + Engineer Co-Founder Mixer*

Have a startup idea but missing a builder? Or an engineer looking for that
big idea? Or maybe a PM who wants to team up with founders? This is the
place to meet your future co-founder 👩‍💻👨‍💼💡

✨ *Columbia PMC has joined hands* with Columbia Entrepreneurs Organization,
AI Club, and SPS Consulting Club to host a night of networking across CBS,
SEAS & SPS — connect with entrepreneurs, PMs, and engineers who are ready
to collaborate and create. Food’s on us, energy’s on you.

📍 *Columbia Business School – Manhattanville Campus, Geffen 350*
📅 Monday, Sept 22 | 6 PM
🍕 Free Food

🔗 Scan the QR code on the flyer or hit the link to RSVP (for SEAS grads):
👉 https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://forms.gle/depWMvp1AoeHq79m7__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmqAXUmGBU$

👩‍🎓 *IEOR students can RSVP through CBS CampusGroups.*

⚠️ *Note:* This RSVP form is only for *SEAS graduate students.* If you fill
this form and are not a SEAS student, your entry will be removed. The
respective schools will be releasing their own RSVP lists.

✅ *Bring your Columbia ID for entry.*

🔔 Follow us for updates & behind-the-scenes:
📸 Instagram: @columbiapmc
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://instagram.com/columbiapmc__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RdRKVzQoC9GJB_lmxwLhaIYBV8TYTOn6vEn7ncC8fqx6sdyvr2szP1vQOwrL4AUpfo_FeKwsfs6rj6rpw46b30vmzBDIp98jAHDDIMA$>

<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.linkedin.com/company/columbia-pmc__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!RdRKVzQoC9GJB_lmxwLhaIYBV8TYTOn6vEn7ncC8fqx6sdyvr2szP1vQOwrL4AUpfo_FeKwsfs6rj6rpw46b30vmzBDIp98j8njUJS4$>
🎯 *Calling all new grads!*
Are you passionate about product, tech consulting, or startups?
Want to be part of the *most happening club at SEAS*, where *every* member
is a go-getter with offers at *startups, FAANG, Bain, BCG, and founder
offices*?

*This is your sign.* 👀

💡 Whether you're obsessed with GenAI, curious about Blockchain, or
dreaming of building your first MVP — *we’ve got a role for you.*

🛠️ *What We Do at Columbia PMC:*
• Host fireside chats, GenAI panels, and product dinners
• Lead hands-on workshops on roadmaps, launches, and growth hacks
• Bring real PMs to campus — from Meta to McKinsey
• Build a shared playbook for cracking tech roles across industries

🌍 This year, we put Columbia PMC *on the map* — and we’re just getting
started.

📝 *Apply if you're:*
✔️ A new grad at SEAS, Columbia University
✔️ Hungry to build, lead, and grow
✔️ Looking to leave your mark on campus and beyond
✔️ (*And yes — we train you. No prior PM experience needed!*)

🚀 *How to Join Us:*

1. *Fill out the form* 📝 – Tell us who you are, what you care about, and
upload your resume

2. *Interview* 🤝 – A quick convo to get to know you

3. *Get Selected *🏅 – We’ll match you to a leadership role that fits your
strengths

4. *Build + Create* 🎉 – Work with us to design game-changing events and
initiatives

🔗 *Form Link* → https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://forms.gle/tfP3xqmbnoq3eC5L8__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmqeTvnTmk$ 
📝 Or scan the QR code in the attached flyer.

📌 *Applications will be closed on Sept 24.*

📸 Instagram: [@columbiapmc](https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://instagram.com/columbiapmc__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmqh09FhAw$ )

Let’s co-build the most innovative, collaborative, and ambitious PMC cohort
Columbia’s ever seen. 💥

With love & launch plans,
*The 2025–2026 Columbia PMC Board* 💙
*CTV, Columbia’s technology transfer office, is hiring graduate students
for the Fellows Program
<https://techventures.columbia.edu/about-ctv/ctv-fellows-program  >*. This is
a unique internship opportunity that introduces scientists to technology
commercialization. Fellows work closely with CTV on a wide range of
projects, including assessment of the commercial viability of new
inventions, identifying potential licensees or investors, developing
marketing materials, and analysis of patent literature. Fellows are paid
$28.46 per hour.

*Applications for the Fellows Program will open 9/17/2025*. The first round
of applications is due *10/2/2025 by 5 pm EDT*. Info sessions will be held
on Sept. 15 at 3 pm and Sept. 18 at 10 am. Both will be Zoom meetings held
on this link: https://columbiauniversity.zoom.us/my/ctvfellows  .

Looking for a hands-on, impactful internship next summer?YC just released
its Summer 2026 internship list, featuring roles at top AI startups where
you’ll work directly with founders, tackle meaningful projects, and gain
first-hand experience building the future of AI. Many past interns have
even gone on to launch their own startups and join YC as founders! Check
out the opportunities below!
YC Summer 2026 Internship List <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://znsrc.com/c/kfmvencctd__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmqD9XkEnQ$ >
*We are thrilled to announce that applications for Nucleate's **Flagship
Activator Program* <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.org/activator/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmqA6mAcfM$ >* are **NOW OPEN!*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmqf3P4cis$ >
Are you an academic trainee (Post-doc/PhD/MBA/MD/JD/MS) eager to build a
life science startup? Whether your passion lies in *therapeutics,
diagnostics, medtech, synthetic biology*, or fields like *food, energy,
agriculture, or consumer products*, this is your opportunity to transform
your research into a groundbreaking venture!
What We Offer:
●      *Completely Free:* Nucleate never takes equity or charges fees for
its programs
●      *Mentorship from Industry Leaders:* Receive guidance from
experienced mentors.
●      *Comprehensive Educational Programming:* Learn from the best in
workshops and seminars.
●      *Global Community: *Join a network of peers, alumni, and leaders
across 18+ cities worldwide.
●      *Investor Introductions:* Get connected with investors ready to
support your breakthrough.
●      *No Forced Incorporation:* Focus on your venture at your own pace.
If you have an idea (i.e., you’re an Inventor), you can apply for either
our *Bio* or *Eco* track:
●      *Bio Track*: For innovators in therapeutics, diagnostics, or tools.
●      *Eco Track*: Focused on food & agriculture, chemicals & energy, or
consumer products.
*Don’t have an idea yet?* No worries! You can still join a team as a
*scientific,
business, or clinical contributor* through our matching process.
*Got a team already?* Your collaborators can come on board as *team
affiliates*, so you all get the chance to learn and build together.
*Applications* <https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmqf3P4cis$ >* are reviewed on a rolling
basis through October 20. Early submission is encouraged for early team
matching*.
*Check out our **FAQs page*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://drive.google.com/file/d/1WV3LQJYHbdauBCrYzPAjDUarJPJGrK5a/view__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmqymqBTEA$ >* if
you have any questions*
To learn more, join us at one of the following info sessions. Please
register to attend, food and drink will be served!

   1. Columbia Morningside
   <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/64sdaqgf?utm_source=columbia-group__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmqQVj8u-s$ >Info Session:

   30, 5:30 PM at Davis Auditorium
   2. Columbia Medical Center
   <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/xwnoz3hs?utm_source=columbia-group__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmqFPztitc$ > Info Session:

   2, 5:30 PM at Alexandria LaunchLabs

Can’t make the in-person session? *Sign up for a virtual info session or
Live Q&A session with Activator Alumni and Mentors*:

   <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/n5t938v2?utm_source=columbia-group__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmqhB3ic98$ >  Thursday,

   7, at 6 PM

Alternatively, join us at one of our *events*
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://lu.ma/nucleate-ny-community-events__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmq5-8GH9A$ > in NYC!

   1. *Connect and Collab: Fall Mixer with NYAS*
   <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://luma.com/c4fiyh8y?utm_source=columbia-group__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmq-JMWPFU$ > Thursday,
   September 25th, 6 PM at NYAS

*What about IP?** Nucleate Activator is an education-focused, non-dilutive
program. We do not take any equity or IP, and all programming is designed
to be non-confidential. Inventors applying to the program must secure
approval from their PI and TTO before final acceptance.*
*About Nucleate*
Nucleate <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.xyz/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmqiQlMDFQ$ > is a student-run nonprofit organization
that facilitates the formation of new life science ventures. Our
equity-free Activator program <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://nucleate.xyz/activator/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmqwZrAcek$ > supports
emerging bioentrepreneurs coming from academia who plan to tackle the
biggest challenges in human health (Bio Track) and sustainability (Eco
Track) by building life science companies. Since our inception seven years
ago, we have guided 600+ scientific teams through their earliest stages,
leading to the formation of more than 180 companies that have collectively
raised over $500M. Our alumni are commercializing breakthrough technologies
such as protein sequencing (Glyphic <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://glyphic.bio/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmqb7g8c5M$ >), AI drug design
(Manifold Bio <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.manifold.bio/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmqI7C-lRs$ >, Concerto
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.concertobio.com/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmq8pp0nwc$ >), biomanufacturing (Foray Biosciences
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://foraybio.com/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmq0hajuYY$ >) and many more.
*Apply*
Apply here <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://apply.nucleate.org/__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!QXVueh-WkBFA8Wuvn0gBf23D6zBfHaadcLLN470eBkBi5EmurKdCRV1Fo3s-He_YdlhP_xTxjtjyTZQcUmQMAPgi39Gu5XXuPwDnqWFvbYmqVpF8UuU$ > before *October 20th, 2025*.
*To Learn more about Nucleate visit our **Linktree*

threads.subject: Share Your Thoughts on Columbia’s Institutional Culture by Friday, Sept. 26
threads.originalFrom: "Angela V. Olinto, Provost of the University & Daniel Driscoll, Vice President of Human Resources" <officeoftheprovost@columbia.edu>

messages.from: "Angela V. Olinto, Provost of the University & Daniel Driscoll, Vice President of Human Resources" <officeoftheprovost@columbia.edu>
messages.subject: Share Your Thoughts on Columbia’s Institutional Culture by Friday, Sept. 26
messages.body:
September 23, 2025

Dear Columbia University Faculty and Staff,

We still want to hear about your workplace climate experience. If you have not yet shared your perspective, we encourage you to take 15 minutes today to complete Columbia’s Employee Climate and Engagement Survey before it closes on Friday, September 26.

All responses will remain confidential. Results will be reported only in aggregate form and will not identify individual participants.

https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=9cacdc3dd0286bedf387cd45ff93164300584704a632bf1cbdf44b0a113021cc28381c6678939115431760c366336260bc0ca9b4b795049dee84f5bb108a8bf9  
Complete the Survey Now 

For more details, including FAQs, visit the 
https://click.m.columbia.edu/?qs=9cacdc3dd0286bedba7c9db3af1317bc8c6726afd83bff7e859cf145f5d1350784f89bed09bdff9603b50360a116ad72ebf21ba57af0242c67c1dfcc0a30bf57  
Climate and Engagement Survey website  or contact 
mailto:employee-climate-survey@columbia.edu?subject=A%20question%20about%20the%20Climate%20and%20Engagement%20Survey 
employee-climate-survey@columbia.edu  with any questions.

Thank you for adding your voice to this important University-wide effort.

Best,

Angela V. Olinto

Provost

Rutherfurd Professor of Astronomy

Professor of Physics

Dan Driscoll

Chief Human Resources Officer

Copyright (c) 2025 Columbia University. All rights reserved.You are receiving this email because you are part of the Columbia University community.

Columbia University

116th and Broadway

New York, NY 10027

threads.subject: Upcoming October IDP Sessions – Flyers Attached

messages.subject: Upcoming October IDP Sessions – Flyers Attached
messages.body:
Dear Doctoral Students,

We’ve got some great Individual Development Plan (IDP) sessions coming up
this October! These workshops are designed to help you strengthen key
skills for your Ph.D. journey and career development.

Attached are flyers with details. Registration links are also below for
easy access:

   - October 1: How to Approach and Connect with Industry Professionals
   <https://events.columbia.edu/go/IndustryConversations2025  >
      - This session offers an overview of informational interviewing and
      its vital role in the career planning and exploration process.
participants
      will learn how to identify and reach out to potential contacts, structure
      effective conversations, and ask insightful questions to gather
meaningful
      career insights. The session will also address key social norms and
      strategies for making informational interviews
relationship-focused rather
      than transactional. Additionally, it will provide guidance on
how to follow
      up and maintain connections to support ongoing professional growth.
   - October 3: Presentation Skills for Scholars: Clarity, Confidence &
   Connection <https://events.columbia.edu/go/Presenting2025  >
      - Presentation Skills for Scholars: Clarity, Confidence & Connection
      is an in-person, 90-minute workshop for graduate students and postdocs on
      how to give an effective class presentation, defend a thesis, or
present a
      research project.
   - October 21: Telling Your Story: Stand Out in Career Conversations
   <https://events.columbia.edu/go/TellingYourStory  >
      - In this engaging workshop, participants will learn how to use
      storytelling as a powerful tool for networking and interviewing. Through
      guided exercises and active listening, you will craft a clear and
      compelling narrative that communicates your strengths, skills, values and
      professional journey. Whether you're preparing for informational
      interviews, job interviews, or professional networking events,
this session
      will help you articulate your story in ways that resonate with diverse
      audiences and career paths. This workshop has been adapted from materials
      in the Professional Development Hub (pd|hub) Collections, developed with
      funding from the NIH.
   - October 24: Working with Workplace Communication Styles
   <https://events.columbia.edu/go/CommunicationStyles  >
      - Effective communication is essential for building trust, providing
      feedback, and fostering collaboration in mentoring and team-based
      environments. This workshop introduces an established framework to help
      participants understand their own and others' communication
styles and how
      they impact team dynamics and mentoring relationships.

These sessions are a great opportunity to practice professional skills,
gain insights, and connect with peers.

Best,
*Richard Hagen, Ph.D.* *(He/Him)*
Assistant Director, Graduate Student Affairs
Engineering Student Affairs
Columbia Engineering

students in mind that you think

ZjQcmQRYFpfptBannerEnd

Hi there,

We are thrilled to share with you an invite only virtual informational
event for our Applied Research Internship Program. The event will take
place on September 29 at 3:30-4:30pm EST. If you have any students in mind
that you think might be a good fit for this program, please send them this
information.

This event will include general information about the program, life at
Capital One, followed by the opportunity to hear from Bayan Bruss
<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://www.linkedin.com/jobs/__;!!EFVe01R3CjU!ZkW-SyBWcRd80WDxCG3TLFgZ5FSn7yRqzGDyT-S0RZQC0oCtiaudMJjgW_Ynhdsrj6NuZFfF4iTXxpJOx5_vip0CuFxHitLZXrI$>,
VP, Applied Research. For more information about the event and how to
register, see the attached flyer.

About the Program:

This program is a 12-week PhD-level internship in which interns conduct
high impact applied research within the latest AI developments. Interns
will have the opportunity to work towards publications at top conferences,
access VP level knowledge, and expand their network. As we are at the
beginning of our recruiting cycle for summer 2027, we hope you can join us
next Monday to learn more about the program!

If you have any questions, please reach out to @Bari Goldschein

Thank you!

Katie

-- 
[image: Capital One]

Katie Clark

Senior Manager • Academic Partnerships
Intelligent Foundations & Experiences (IFX) COS
West Creek 4

messages.from: Columbia Business School Behavioral Research Lab <columbia-brl-admin@sona-systems.net>
messages.subject: New Timeslots and Online Study Announcement: Columbia Business School Behavioral Research Lab
messages.body:
Dear Participants, 

Welcome to the new semester! We are excited to share that several study opportunities are now available on SONA: 

Velmara Study – In-Person (Uris Hall BRL)
 Participate in a quick 7-min study. After completing a simple task, fill out a short Qualtrics survey and you will receive a $5 Amazon gift card.

Sign up here: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia-brl.sona-systems.com/default.aspx?p_return_experiment_id=1171__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!W1TgLTEwU9VtFEMpQCNTwVIHdYqVV124vdGLsZcsfd3cl6NPyO0IsNvHC_x92EzPdbtXe_bzwP5BAxk2fRnXlff7U_lERCgCevp1SA$   

Cognitive Tasks – In-Person (Uris Hall BRL)
 The study is recruiting participants to complete a 5-minute study where they will be asked to complete a task and take a survey. Each participant will receive $5 Amazon gift card, but can make up to $10. 

Sign up here: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia-brl.sona-systems.com/default.aspx?p_return_experiment_id=1166__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!W1TgLTEwU9VtFEMpQCNTwVIHdYqVV124vdGLsZcsfd3cl6NPyO0IsNvHC_x92EzPdbtXe_bzwP5BAxk2fRnXlff7U_lERCg4qUP3kA$  

New York Runner Survey – Online
 The study is surveying how runners in NYC feel in real-time. Open the survey right before a run. Read the instructions until you reach the STOP page. Then, go on your run. After you finish, return to the survey to answer a few follow-ups about how you felt. The study takes 3 minutes and you will receive $5 Amazon gift card.

Sign up here: https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://columbia-brl.sona-systems.com/default.aspx?p_return_experiment_id=1178__;!!BDUfV1Et5lrpZQ!W1TgLTEwU9VtFEMpQCNTwVIHdYqVV124vdGLsZcsfd3cl6NPyO0IsNvHC_x92EzPdbtXe_bzwP5BAxk2fRnXlff7U_lERChmVM-z4g$  

--- 

Log in to SONA to view all available timeslots. Spots are limited, so we encourage you to sign up early. 

Thank you for being part of our participant pool - your contributions make our research possible. 

Best regards,
 Behavioral Research Lab Team

XDBFAD-TDBFAD-SDBFAD-OEQRGGDGWBFAD-EWGSEFSWQWBFE-PBADQBR-MDBDSQ

threads.subject: Action Required: Columbia Student Health Insurance Plan

messages.subject: Action Required: Columbia Student Health Insurance Plan
messages.body:
Dear Student,

The Student Health Insurance Office has notified GSAS that you have not taken action to either enroll or waive in the student health insurance plan for the 2025-2026 plan year. The end of the open enrollment period and the deadline to take action is Tuesday, September 30.

  1.  If you wish to waive the plan, please go onto your patient portal<https://cas.columbia.edu/cas/login?service=https%3a%2f%2fsecure.health.columbia.edu > and complete the waiver request and upload supporting documentation for review.

  1.  If you plan to enroll in the plan, please go onto your patient portal<https://cas.columbia.edu/cas/login?service=https%3a%2f%2fsecure.health.columbia.edu > and select enroll and acknowledge the statements in the agreement section.

In doing either, you will ensure your billing is correct. If you do not take any action by September 30, you will be automatically enrolled in the plan and billed accordingly.

Thank you for taking a minute to complete your submission. Please reach out to studentinsurance@columbia.edu<mailto:studentinsurance@columbia.edu> with any questions or concerns.

Sincerely,

GSAS Office of Student Affairs

17-Sep-2025

Dear Zhang, Shiyan; McKinnon-Crowley, Jocelyn; Cai, Alice; Sreedhar, Karthik; Chilton, Lydia B.; Nickerson, Jeffrey V.:

Your revised manuscript entitled "Permeable Artifacts and Synthetic Organizational Forms: 
Action Design Research for the Institutional Press" has been successfully resubmitted online and is presently being considered for publication in MIS Quarterly.

Your manuscript ID is 2024-SI-19806.R1.

Please refer to the above manuscript ID in all future correspondence regarding your paper. If there is a change in your e-mail address, please log in to ScholarOne Manuscripts at https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__mc.manuscriptcentral.com_misq&d=DwICaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=nVJSmOEALCqsqncBv-PxA7i2fmcG7mQtNYOW8b3--210cCyxod1Fia_KGYYrVn8U&s=of0xvY8EZ3TBGdXASybmHkQix4ZFkC3mwyrOAM_q5b8&e= and edit your user information as appropriate.

messages.from: Columbia Business School Behavioral Research Lab <columbia-brl-admin@sona-systems.net>
messages.subject: New Study Announcement: Columbia Business School Behavioral Research Lab
messages.body:
Dear Participants, 

There is a new Zoom study available. It is a LIVE-online group study with experimenter on giving & receiving feedback. This study will last ~45 minutes and each participant will receive a $20.00 US bank pay card for completing it. 

Sign up for timeslots via this link: https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__columbia-2Dbrl.sona-2Dsystems.com_default.aspx-3Fp-5Freturn-5Fexperiment-5Fid-3D1175&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=uwxyT4N2v7HA4r_ExhLNsEN0t1NvU78y-jJuALvSwMO1gk1m5eb867dNTV6adwg2&s=6Ov8zWOyxEUjnL_lYUbFryaFVaQxf5XcBnRp4qm92V4&e=  

Thank you for participating! 

- The BRL Team

XDBFAD-TDBFAD-SDBFAD-OEQRGGDGWBFAD-EWGSEFSERSGSD-PBADQBR-MDBDSQ

messages.subject: Nomination for Membership
messages.body:
View [ https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__SX.informz.net_InformzDataService_OnlineVersion_Individual-3FmailingInstanceId-3D12104965-26subscriberId-3D1208129661&d=DwIBaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=fSBKIDQ0V4UWHyHOsGmkBoRSKVTKZQT7BXUYeRXPbUVqYBMnx7vNWdxIOGY6utKM&s=kxJZ-6Na0LO0ELiux1dMQjfFb8ON6sH-13aunBSGD-A&e=  ]  in your browser.

 Dear Karthik Sreedhar,
In recognition of your scholarly achievements and contributions to the advancement of knowledge in your field, it is my pleasure to inform you that you have been nominated for Full Membership in Sigma Xi, The Scientific Research Honor Society [ https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=http-3A__sigmaxi.org&d=DwIBaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=fSBKIDQ0V4UWHyHOsGmkBoRSKVTKZQT7BXUYeRXPbUVqYBMnx7vNWdxIOGY6utKM&s=nS0aR512uwSHgRA5DUy4SCrNv-FVbThUibXnh7jcq-I&e=  ] . Congratulations!
Members enjoy access to programs and initiatives that recognize innovation, support interdisciplinary research, advance careers for STEM students and professionals, and promote the public understanding of science. Additional benefits include a subscription to Sigma Xi's award-winning magazine, American Scientist [ https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=http-3A__americanscientist.org&d=DwIBaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=fSBKIDQ0V4UWHyHOsGmkBoRSKVTKZQT7BXUYeRXPbUVqYBMnx7vNWdxIOGY6utKM&s=8FZj_y8RtLRpLgYJP3n3VaU5T_iHEogNak3t-cH4wG4&e=  ] , and exclusive member rates for the Society's annual conference, the International Forum on Research Excellence (IFoRE) [ https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=http-3A__experienceifore.org&d=DwIBaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=fSBKIDQ0V4UWHyHOsGmkBoRSKVTKZQT7BXUYeRXPbUVqYBMnx7vNWdxIOGY6utKM&s=ZRcX0yUW7ZOC8ehjUwvO8hNZ_G90rKsW0t2yl6hHgUU&e=  ] .
 
 [ https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.experienceifore.org&d=DwIBaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=fSBKIDQ0V4UWHyHOsGmkBoRSKVTKZQT7BXUYeRXPbUVqYBMnx7vNWdxIOGY6utKM&s=sULgPKrZxhgsqy2OhfxXU9MEy8C5oCSLEZ1ojiSRcZI&e=  ] 
 img border="0" height="142" src="https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__sx.informz.net_SX_data_images_IFoRE-5Fcopytext-5Fheader-5F600-2520-28002-29.jpg&d=DwIBaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=fSBKIDQ0V4UWHyHOsGmkBoRSKVTKZQT7BXUYeRXPbUVqYBMnx7vNWdxIOGY6utKM&s=29kzdISPEdpgxXY0EOJ3mNHQxumXoV0anbWPDdI3MRo&e= " style="border: 0px; height: 142px; width: 500px; vertical-align: middle; display: block; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;" valign="middle" width="500"  [ https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.experienceifore.org&d=DwIBaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=fSBKIDQ0V4UWHyHOsGmkBoRSKVTKZQT7BXUYeRXPbUVqYBMnx7vNWdxIOGY6utKM&s=sULgPKrZxhgsqy2OhfxXU9MEy8C5oCSLEZ1ojiSRcZI&e=  ] 
 [ https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.experienceifore.org&d=DwIBaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=fSBKIDQ0V4UWHyHOsGmkBoRSKVTKZQT7BXUYeRXPbUVqYBMnx7vNWdxIOGY6utKM&s=sULgPKrZxhgsqy2OhfxXU9MEy8C5oCSLEZ1ojiSRcZI&e=  ] 
 
Don’t miss the opportunity to attend Sigma Xi’s annual conference,  iFoRE , where innovation, collaboration, and discovery come to life. This is your chance to connect with fellow student researchers from around the world and engage with leading scientists who are shaping the future of science. Our iFoRE 2024 featured inspiring keynote speakers, including renowned experts like   Cato T. Laurencin, MD, Ph.D. ,  Peter J. Hotez ,  MD, Ph.D., Adrianna Bankston MD, Ph.D.  , and many more pioneers driving groundbreaking advancements in research. Become a member now and join us to expand your network, share your ideas, and be part of a vibrant scientific community committed to advancing knowledge and innovation in 2025!
Please follow the link below to accept your nomination. If you have any questions about Sigma Xi, feel free to reply directly to this email. Please visit sigmaxi.org [ https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=http-3A__sigmaxi.org&d=DwIBaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=fSBKIDQ0V4UWHyHOsGmkBoRSKVTKZQT7BXUYeRXPbUVqYBMnx7vNWdxIOGY6utKM&s=nS0aR512uwSHgRA5DUy4SCrNv-FVbThUibXnh7jcq-I&e=  ]  to learn more about the Society's leadership, members, programs, awards, and publications. Once again, congratulations on this exceptional honor. I look forward to welcoming you as a companion in zealous research!
 [ https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.sigmaxi.org_nom-2Daccept-2Ddp-2Dfull&d=DwIBaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=fSBKIDQ0V4UWHyHOsGmkBoRSKVTKZQT7BXUYeRXPbUVqYBMnx7vNWdxIOGY6utKM&s=iOi-6HbQYIBmtMjq7YRKVO2vBLA6WXALgyUEvdq_w4E&e=  ] 
 [ https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.sigmaxi.org_nom-2Daccept-2Ddp-2Dfull&d=DwIBaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=fSBKIDQ0V4UWHyHOsGmkBoRSKVTKZQT7BXUYeRXPbUVqYBMnx7vNWdxIOGY6utKM&s=iOi-6HbQYIBmtMjq7YRKVO2vBLA6WXALgyUEvdq_w4E&e=  ]

threads.subject: Last Chance for Boat Cruise Tickets! | EGSC Weekly Newsletter

messages.subject: Last Chance for Boat Cruise Tickets! | EGSC Weekly Newsletter
messages.body:
<https://egsc.engineering.columbia.edu/  >
*Hey Grad SEAS!*

Just *10 days* to go until our *Fall 2025 Boat Cruise!* If you haven’t
grabbed your ticket yet, now’s the time! *Today is your last chance!*

*Department Representative Elections close tonight at 11:59 PM! *Don’t
forget to cast your vote! Plus, join us for* EGSC Cocoa House this
Wednesday *and our first* General Body Meeting this Friday!*

Don't forget to *follow us on Instagram @columbiaegsc
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.instagram.com_columbiaegsc_&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=Cb_CSEMAkvP3tyUqTDqTw4Y24I3JZggiCZhfegGkp9g&m=yKg8xkw82qhm0wl6Rw6tgso6BKaoJHzNftfLDVHlqUamp8fH3Q-kUamuyeaW1E5u&s=TtS8AkX6xdlQ6Gju0VA-_O-ykTVh-JM2Q8kiRxIH3nU&e= > *to stay updated on all our
events!
Pictures from our first Carleton Commons Social are here!
Check Out the Album!
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__drive.google.com_drive_folders_1VA7nSEIkvuGD6InHdKxuHWBMY7uol30k-3Fusp-3Dsharing&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=Cb_CSEMAkvP3tyUqTDqTw4Y24I3JZggiCZhfegGkp9g&m=yKg8xkw82qhm0wl6Rw6tgso6BKaoJHzNftfLDVHlqUamp8fH3Q-kUamuyeaW1E5u&s=HFe63ZZw77muRhwogN49WDGVazgdDScUS8hbGrT1tAk&e= >

*Department Representative Elections are live! *Cast your vote and help
choose the candidates who will *represent you* and *your department! *The
email regarding election information was sent yesterday!
Mark your calendars—*September 26th* is set for an unforgettable night on
the water! Join us for an evening of stunning skyline views, great music,
and endless memories as we set sail on the *Fall 2025 EGSC Boat Cruise*!

*TICKET RELEASE SCHEDULE:*

   - *SEPTEMBER 15 (5PM): *General Admission 1
   - *SEPTEMBER 16 (3PM): *General Admission 2

Want to hear your favorite songs while dancing on a boat? Request them here
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__forms.gle_bvTe77Gw4rThx4bk7&d=DwMFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=Cb_CSEMAkvP3tyUqTDqTw4Y24I3JZggiCZhfegGkp9g&m=qAGIZSj-vvk5gTYUbBR2E5MvK5jQrEsY_sxJkmBA8ntbSfHUUnxPVAWVhwy84lRC&s=TsWqJctkV3qC2SyzMLYm7zubobQEsuadeX3r9Xrt0bg&e=>
so

*CTV, Columbia’s technology transfer office, is hiring graduate students

on Sept. 15 at 3 pm and Sept. 18 at 10 am. Both will be Zoom meetings held
on this link: https://columbiauniversity.zoom.us/my/ctvfellows  .

life science startup? Whether your passion lies in *therapeutics,

●      *Completely Free:* Nucleate never takes equity or charges fees for
its programs
●      *Mentorship from Industry Leaders:* Receive guidance from
experienced mentors.

support your breakthrough.

our *Bio* or *Eco* track:

●      *Eco Track*: Focused on food & agriculture, chemicals & energy, or
consumer products.

program. We do not take any equity or IP, and all programming is designed

*About Nucleate*
Nucleate <https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__nucleate.xyz_&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=Cb_CSEMAkvP3tyUqTDqTw4Y24I3JZggiCZhfegGkp9g&m=yKg8xkw82qhm0wl6Rw6tgso6BKaoJHzNftfLDVHlqUamp8fH3Q-kUamuyeaW1E5u&s=iY03iCXIQB7JK6BkiM-cmITAx8BiBdA8uJHTwggtKi4&e= > is a student-run nonprofit organization

equity-free Activator program <https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__nucleate.xyz_activator_&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=Cb_CSEMAkvP3tyUqTDqTw4Y24I3JZggiCZhfegGkp9g&m=yKg8xkw82qhm0wl6Rw6tgso6BKaoJHzNftfLDVHlqUamp8fH3Q-kUamuyeaW1E5u&s=MPhKfS5qHBCoUP9WNRqO9y4MnszbkuxRtoZLpeKf1NU&e= > supports

threads.subject: MongoDB System Alert: Mongorestore silently ignores namespaces containing newlines

messages.subject: MongoDB System Alert: Mongorestore silently ignores namespaces containing newlines
messages.body:
Dear MongoDB Customer,

We have identified a critical issue in mongorestore, a utility included in 
MongoDB Database Tools. If a database or collection name contains a newline 
character (ASCII Line Feed, \n, 0x0a), mongorestore will silently skip 
restoring it, without logging any warnings.

You are not impacted by this issue if you:

 * 
Do not have any databases or collections with newline characters in their 
names; or

 * 
Never used mongorestore to restore a database or collection from a mongodump 
backup.

MongoDB Database Tools v100.12.2 already contains fixes for this issue.

This issue can only manifest if BOTH of the following are true:

 * You have a database or collection with a name containing a newline 
character (this is not common); and
 * Mongorestore v3.3.11–v100.12.1 was used to restore the database or 
collection.
If your MongoDB namespaces contain newline characters and you believe you 
could be affected due to the criteria above, we recommend that you:

 * Verify which databases and collections are missing.
 * If you have access to a previous backup, restore any missing databases or 
collections using the latest version of mongorestore in MongoDB Database Tools 
v100.12.2 or later.
For more information and examples of the above steps, please refer to 
TOOLS-3822 <https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__jira.mongodb.org_browse_TOOLS-2D3822&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=FdwLMKGvOsipoMucRs1wJh7IRVZ0C3em3WMHSolpKrMl47kgJ9h7G8SYaJi4MlTP&s=OyE99wYdJzXG0oxjE02fg2POUOWCQr6Kb5Mdf-juo88&e= >.

The latest version of the MongoDB Database Tools can be downloaded here: 
https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.mongodb.com_try_download_database-2Dtools&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=FdwLMKGvOsipoMucRs1wJh7IRVZ0C3em3WMHSolpKrMl47kgJ9h7G8SYaJi4MlTP&s=LzoNMigNLBrVEGrrb0TMnLbd3_jyJ-K1IIzRBQNvljU&e=

If you have any questions, please open a support case 
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__support.mongodb.com_welcome&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=FdwLMKGvOsipoMucRs1wJh7IRVZ0C3em3WMHSolpKrMl47kgJ9h7G8SYaJi4MlTP&s=gZ-__QNx9z_d5EDobDGFenH3P1FGPK2TBt-pBhdXKg8&e= > or start a chat 
<https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__www.mongodb.com_contact&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=2WR0ko0o4Val5uK--6ILz02ebCRgynFBUs9x2LG9Eeg&m=FdwLMKGvOsipoMucRs1wJh7IRVZ0C3em3WMHSolpKrMl47kgJ9h7G8SYaJi4MlTP&s=L041B2ZscC4PCEd_bWvsCQ7B6r11t2f8d38dg7Sa6ss&e= > with the MongoDB Support team.

Sincerely,

The MongoDB Team

threads.subject: EGSC Department Representative Voting Open Now!! Until 11:59 PM tomorrow, Sep 16

messages.subject: EGSC Department Representative Voting Open Now!! Until 11:59 PM tomorrow, Sep 16
messages.body:
Dear Graduate SEAS:

Elections for Fall 2025 Department Representatives are now open! These
representatives will be critical in advocating on your behalf within EGSC
and in planning events for all Grad SEAS! As such, we want YOU to decide
who is best suited to represent you!

Voting is open now through 11:59 PM EDT tomorrow Tuesday, September 16th.
See the links below for each department’s voting form (candidate bios are
linked in each form).

Please note: login with your Columbia email and vote ONLY for candidates in
your own department (e.g., if you are a CS student, please only cast votes
in the CS election). Votes cast by students for departments in which they
are not members will be nullified.

There are no elections for Applied Physics & Applied Math (APAM), Civil
Engineering and Engineering Mechanics (CEEM), Executive MBA - MS Dual
Degree (MBAxMS), or Mechanical Engineering (MechE) as the number of
applicants is less than or equal to the number of available positions.

Thank you for your help, and please reach out with any questions, comments,
or concerns to president@egsc.seas.columbia.edu!

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Biomedical Engineering (BME) <https://urldefense.proofpoint.com/v2/url?u=https-3A__forms.gle_dQkSHasb6Mw41j9k7&d=DwIFaQ&c=009klHSCxuh5AI1vNQzSO0KGjl4nbi2Q0M1QLJX9BeE&r=ul8QkDqRh_NYIgxWcCfEU6pkVKR221Kvf0vUqobyKbJhd1zj0iTCniRzauKT4TdE&m=dPTF09uXXpjIu5z6XHwWmzEtW9G-vzIlGQZHjumNXlBX3TIgiPFhp0HlrK2FKGQo&s=5gykKWHXxZBcPary4k2YN82eP9txfepmwyX-J51BkMo&e= >
